allContractsIncentivized: Funding Address: 0xece69B4a3E77952e615426b95E42b68E6e4a56aE; Reward handler: 0xece69B4a3E77952e615426b95E42b68E6e4a56aE
phase1_statsChanges:
We have updated on May 29th to the latest incentive plan.
Previous Allocation:
Acquisition: 30%
Activation LP: 20%
Activation Trade: 30%
Referral: 20%

New Allocation:
Acquisition: 36%
Activation_LP: 14%
Activation_Trade: 50%

Over the past month, we identified instances of self-referrals. We introduced a minimum trading volume requirement for referees to ensure that referrers only receive fee rebates if the referred wallets meet the volume threshold. Further analysis revealed a cluster of wallets referring each other. While we could implement new rules to prevent this, there is a risk of unforeseen loopholes. Therefore, we are reallocating incentives to prioritize the Activation Trade segment, as referrals and payouts are directly tied to trading volume.

Regarding LP incentives, we have partnered with Stakestone and will receive their points on Aark. This partnership strengthens our LP incentives, ensuring that rewards are based on fees generated by traders rather than direct team incentives. This adjustment is healthier for LPs, aligning rewards with trading activity.

Another matter is Aark decides to align with STIP.b and LTIPP rebate cap on trading fee of <75%. The benchmark follows GMX, Vertex, Synthetix and MUX, to make sure DAO’s funds are better spent. Arbitrum perp protocols will collectively make Arbitrum better!
phase1_statsLessons:
During the 1st period, Aark team detected 3 issues and we will dynamically adjust our strategy in a more systematic way for further distribution. 
 	
Issue 1: Transparency. Since our reward handler contract can only indirectly reflect Galxe user data, we will upload the Galxe data manually per quest ends.
Issue 2. Lack of Exposure. In the incoming periods, Aark will partner with projects embedded with multi-chain features and involve more KoLs with a better reach in the target chains.
Issue 3: Hurdle for Eligibility. In the next period, we will adjust the participation requirement (e.g. targeted acquisition protocols, min deposit) to target more users.
phase1_actualsSummary:
Our incentives plan is breakdown into 4 parts
1/ Acquisition (Galxe): Aark is rewarding users from other Perp DEXs (Aevo, ApeX, ApolloX, Kwenta, KiloEX, Level Finance, and SynFutures) to trade on Aark. So far 1 eligible trader.
2/ Acquisition (DappOS Premium Competition): Aark partnered with DappOS to reward users who trade on Aark through DappOS's multi-chain wallet based on trading volume. So far 3157 traders made $29.9M trading volume.
3/ Activation (Trade): Aark is rewarding traders based on trading volumes. So far 148 made $343M trading volume.
4/ Activation (LP): Aark is rewarding LP based on the size of LP holdings relative to TVL with minimum LP size of $2,000. So far we have 44 eligible users.

1/ Acquisition: Galxe Event for other Perp DEX traders  
This is an exclusive Galxe campaign for users who have executed on-chain transactions worth more than or equal to $50 at Perp DEXs below. There will be a total of 12 campaigns and each will last for a week. The purpose is to catalyze the growth of Arbitrum ecosystem by bringing users from other chains:
Aevo, ApeX, ApolloX, Kwenta, KiloEX, Level Finance, and SynFutures.
Allocation: 17,000 $ARB per week
Duration: June 13th - June 20th.
For details on the event: https://aarkdigital.medium.com/aark-arb-incentive-program-3-calling-perp-dex-traders-to-moon-mode-6b93a11d1120
Eligible Users: 1


2/ Acquisition(1-2): DappOS Premium Competition
Traders, making trading volumes through DappOS, receive $ARB based on their trading volumes. DappOS is a multi-chain wallet which allows users from different chains to use Aark.
Allocation: 50,000 $ARB for 2 weeks
Duration: June 4th - June 18th.
For details on the event: https://medium.com/@dappos.com/premium-trading-competition-cb0da7ad13c1
Eligible Users: 3157 traders who made $29.9M trading volume in total.


3/ Activation_Trade: Aark’s Native Event for Traders.
By trading with Classic Mode and Moon Mode on Aark, traders receive $ARB based on their weekly trading volumes. There will be a total of 12 weeks of epoch.
Allocation: 37,500 $ARB per week
Duration: June 5th - June 12th
For details on the event:
https://aarkdigital.medium.com/aark-new-token-mining-unveil-j-3cdfe2d36771
Eligible Users: 148 who made $343M trading volume in total.


4/ Activation_LP: Aark’s Native Event for LPs.
LPs earn $ARB weekly, based on their LST/LRT average collateral size for a week relative to the total TVL of LST/LRT deposits in the LP pool. To be eligible, users must maintain their LP size more than or equal to $2000. There will be a total of 12 weeks of epoch. 
Allocation: 10,500 $ARB per week
Duration: June 5th - June 12th
For details on the event:
https://aarkdigital.medium.com/aark-arb-incentive-program-for-liquidity-providers-21bd8b6a76f3
Eligible Users: 44 users

- Activation (LP): Aark is rewarding LP based on the size of LP holdings relative to TVL with minimum LP size of $2,000. 10,500 $ARB per week. So far we have 37 eligible users with a avg TVL of $7.6M.
phase5_plannedChanges:
For the remaining epochs, we plan on executing various events to acquire new users in different areas.

1. We are running a Moon Listing Event, during which Aark lists assets from the top 20 on Binance Perps in volume. During the first week, we acquired 50 new users who took up 50% of the total trading volumes last week. We achieved this performance with a 10k $ARB allocation; therefore, for the next 2 weeks, we will extend the budget to reach out to a bigger audience to acquire more users.

2. We'll launch a Layer3 quest campaign that requires users to execute on-chain actions on Aark. Considering that Layer3 is one of the most renowned quest platforms, we expect to acquire at least 3-5k participants. With the synergy of the ongoing Moon Listing Event, we've increased the chance of increasing the retention of those users who are funneled by the Layer3 event.

3. We've just launched a trading competition collaborating with Tethermax and dappOS. For 2 weeks from now on, participants will have the chance to be rewarded with $ARB based on their trading volume. The total budget allocated is 50k ARB. Tethermax's userbase is heavily focused on CEX traders, and dappOS provides a multichain wallet that allows users access to services from other chains. With these attributes of partners, we are expanding our exposure to a new segment of users who are expected to create a sizeable volume as they are already familiar with trading.
phase5_plannedExpectations:

phase6_statsLessons: During the 6th peroid, we have learnt from the first two distribution by dynamically adjust our strategy in a more systematic way. phase1_actualsDisclosures:
A portion of LTIP emissions will now be dedicated to Across+ actions, enabling users to deposit into Arbitrum dApps with a single click, regardless of whether their funds are on another chain like OP, Base, or any other Across-supported chains.

This program update will attract more users to Arbitrum dApps by eliminating the need for a separate bridging step, providing a more seamless user experience.

We believe that this creates a positive situation for all stakeholders in the Arbitrum ecosystem due to the following:

Dapps now get access to more liquidity
Users can interact with Arbitrum dapps from anywhere
Arbitrum TVL is more likely to rise due to the chain abstraction within dapps

phase1_statsChanges: no changes
phase1_statsLessons:
The original intent of the grant was to achieve $5m TVL with at least 100 addresses with open positions. Given the price cut of ARB, the expected new goal is $2.5m TVL. Alchemix is already well on its way to these goals, with more marketing/incentives planned to aid in the expansion. 

While TVL is good, the goal is to provide self repaying loans, which requires deep liquidity for alAssets. The uptake on liquidity has not been as easy to obtain as the uptake in TVL. Alchemix is exploring ways to better educate/incentivize liquidity, including potentially migrating some additional liquidity from Mainnet to better support loan-takers.

phase6_statsLessons: TVL has continued to go up. The net yield earned by depositors is in line or below market rates (10% for stables, 15% for eth). This shows that users are not demanding significant yield premium in order to use Alchemix, indicating that Alchemix is an effective way to bring TVL to Arbitrum. The associated dex activity also increase txn/user count.  This supports Alchemix's goal to expand yield offerings on Arbitrum, and confirms that future incentives can be effective.

phase7_statsLessons:
Overall takeaway: Alchemix was able to attract non-negligible TVL with some marketing at reasonable rates. We are continuing to build our additional yield strategies, and have initiated our layer3 quest this week, to re-affirm our commitment to arbitrum despite the program ending. Alchemix was also able to fully distribute the grant on-time, and meet all metric tracking requirements. The ultimate goals were not met, largely due to a significant market downturn that reduced the metrics that the initial application was based on. 
Overall we look forward to continued growth on Arbitrum and look forward to the evolution of the LTIPP process.

phase2_statsChanges: Going forward, we are implementing new measures to protect against Sybil attacks and to remove wallets known to be connected with such activities. Based on the feedback from participants in our Phase 1 leaderboard, we are also implementing changes to how we distribute the pot of $ARB users can win. The changes involve moving from a volume-weighted system to a rank-based system where users who rank high with an overall lower volume could get more rewards. Additionally, we are removing the distribution of sub-1 $ARB as it’s not very meaningful as a reward.

grantSummary:
With this application, Beefy is proposing to deliver a completely novel product first and foremost on the Arbitrum ecosystem, that being our automated liquidity management (“ALM”) product for concentrated liquidity pools. Our ALM product will allow users to gain the benefits of concentrated liquidity, without the cost and risk of managing your own position. With automated rebalancing, Beefy ALM will securely balance each user’s position whilst automatically investing trading fees, giving users certainty of exposure and gain. And unlike many other autocompounding products, our ALM products do not need to sell earned tokens to compound returns. We believe that actively-managed concentrated liquidity is by far the superior form of liquidity for a given ecosystem, and that a push to migrate liquidity on Arbitrum into ALM products will help the chain to become as efficient, liquid and high-yielding as any other major blockchain. We also hope that an effective incentive campaign and launch on Arbitrum will help to attract TVL from our crosschain audience to explore the amazing opportunities available on Arbitrum; win-win-win.

See full application for more details: https://forum.arbitrum.foundation/t/beefy-ltipp-application-draft/21331

phase1_actualsSummary: We have utilized 92,000 ARB to date, and will aim to distribute the remaining 508,000 linearly over the remaining 10-11 weeks. Distribution is working well and has seen significant growth in activity and TVL on Arbitrum for Beefy. So far (as of 21 June), Beefy has amassed $11.3m TVL in Arbitrum CLM products and is well on its way to meeting the targeted $30m of TVL by the end of the LTIPP period.

phase1_plannedChanges: No changes - plans are progressing well as expected. The team have deployed dozens of new Arbitrum products since launch and will rotate incentives to cover as many Arbitrum projects, protocols and tokens as we reasonably can. 17 products were boosted in the first two-week period. For the time being, all rewards are being distributed via Merkl, but other distribution mechanisms for different CLM product types are being prepared.
phase1_plannedExpectations: Carry on targeting a broad range of different products for incentivisation, and developing new CLM product types and incentivization mechanisms.

phase2_statsChanges: No meaningful changes planned. The precise products for incentivization remain under consideration, but distribution is planned via the core Arbitrum Merkl Distribution Contract, which has been provided.
phase2_statsLessons: As our processes have improved, the distribution of rewards in a more granular fashion, across more products, with more variety and for differing periods is allowing us to more effectively capture TVL and offer attractive yields to support particular ecosystem projects and products.
phase2_actualsSummary: We utilized 136,000 ARB to date, holding back some quantity due to the gradual release of new products coming in the following weeks. Distribution continues to work effectively, allowing us to increase the range and frequency of distributions across more pools and more limited periods. As of 1 July, we have reached $13.8 million of TVL, against our target increase of $30 million over the entire period (c. 46% of total target).

phase2_plannedChanges: No meaningful changes planned. The precise products for incentivization remain under consideration, but distribution is planned via the core Arbitrum Merkl Distribution Contract, which has been provided.
phase2_plannedExpectations: To continue shipping large quantities of Arbitrum CLM products and incentivizing a full range of products to cover the ecosystems many projects, protocols and tokens. We will also continue to develop new forms of CLM products and new forms of distribution for incentives, ideally all for use during the LTIPP period.
phase3_statsChanges: Moved from using Merkl Distribution to targetting individual Beefy CLM Pools, which have been designed to distribute incentives directly and without charging any fees to maximize utilization.
phase3_statsLessons: In-house distribution contracts work very well and improve performance and utilisation. Further iterations will follow in the coming weeks.
phase3_actualsSummary: We have used 184,500 ARB to date, again retaining some additional quantity for new product releases expected imminently. Distribution continued to improve in efficacy, with more granular rewards. In particular, the move to Beefy CLM Pools has worked extremely well, offering an end-to-end solution that works best for the CLM products. As of 15 July, we reached $17.9 million in TVL (just under 60% of target), indicating that we remain ahead of target.

phase3_plannedChanges: Moved from using Merkl Distribution to targetting individual Beefy CLM Pools, which have been designed to distribute incentives directly and without charging any fees to maximize utilization.
phase3_plannedExpectations: To effectively switch to in-house distribution contracts and continue increasing the scale, pace and dexterity of distribution techniques.
phase4_statsChanges: Added CLM Vaults in addition to CLM Pools, to compound external incentives and minimise the amount of manual labour required from users to manage and profit from their positions.
phase4_statsLessons: Varied product line of basic managed positions and complex vaults are successful at attracting a range of users and retaining separate types of users in each. Vaults are creating considerable excitement, leading to sharp TVL increases.
phase4_actualsSummary: We have used 268,750 ARB to date, again retaining some additional quantity for new product releases. The scope of distribution continued to expand with the addition of the first CLM Vaults, providing a complete solution to handling all rewards at every layer of the liquidity stack. As new products have gained attention, previously incentivized products have been effective at retaining yield. As of 29 July, we reached $34.9 million in TVL, easily exceeding our target for the entire 12-week campaign period. We now look forward to seeing how much further we can stretch beyond our realistic estimate for the grant, to demonstrate the value that CLM brings to the ecosystem.

phase4_plannedChanges: Adding CLM Vaults, built upon standard CLM products to compound external rewards (but not Beefy LTIPP incentives). Vaults offer more functionality and flexibility to users, so will be incentivised alongside and in some instances in place of the underlying CLM products.
phase4_plannedExpectations: To effectively adopt vaults and continue the expansion of our incentive distribution techniques. To continue the trajectory of exceeding target TVL gains.
phase5_statsChanges: No substantive changes. Migrated some incentives from existing CLM Pools to new CLM Vaults to encourage greater uptake and more flexible products.
phase5_statsLessons: Vaults are proving extremely effective at attracting and migrating TVL, so further iteration and deployments on this front may help to further expand our efficacy.
phase5_actualsSummary: We have used 393,500 ARB so far, which closes the gap of retained ARB in previous weeks to ensure the full balance is used during the program period. Following the introduction of CLM Vaults in the previous period, we have doubled down on the deployment of these new products, issuing dozens more and migrating various of the existing CLM Pool incentives into Vaults instead. As of 12 August 2024, our LTIPP dashboard shows total TVL at $39.2m, now far exceeding our total goal for the campaign period by around 30%. We're thrilled to be delivering outsized return for the Arbitrum ecosystem, and look forward to finishing the campaign in style. 😎

phase5_plannedChanges: Firming up approach to CLM Vaults. Increased automation of reward distribution transaction data to increase the speed, versatility and flexibility of our distribution approaches. Launch further co-incentives with new partners.
phase5_plannedExpectations: To continue the rapid rollout of CLM Vaults and CLM Pools, with granular incentives cleverly allocated across the two to push far beyond our target TVL (which has already been achieved).
phase6_statsLessons: The overall strategy of focusing on lots of small, short-term distributions across the ecosystem continues to pay dividends, reflecting the sophistication of the campaign and the significant potential in the new CLM products.
phase6_actualsSummary: We have used 481,000 ARB so far, leaving a manageable amount remaining for use in the final period. Our strategy of executing massive quantities of boosts (more than can be inputted in this form!) in small and short-term doses across the ecosystem continues to pay dividends. As of 26 August 2024, our LTIPP dashboard shows total TVL at $48.8m, again far exceeding our total goal for the campaign period (now by around 60%).
phase6_plannedChanges: No substantive changes. Continued to migrate incentives from CLM Pools to CLM Vaults to encourage greater uptake and more flexible products.
phase6_plannedExpectations: Push TVL attainment to higher heights by focusing on targeted boosts and liquidity migration to optimal earning opportunities.

phase7_statsLessons: This campaign has been a phenomenal experience, and demonstrated the value of (i) ecosystem-wide programs such as this, (ii) frequent, minor, short-term incentive distribution, and (iii) Beefy's new concentrated liquidity products. We are honoured to have taken part and far exceeded our goal. We hope that all participants have enjoyed the process, and that we can return for similar exercises in the future. Thank you to Arbitrum DAO, Offchain Labs, and all those who helped to facilitate this fantastic program!
phase7_actualsSummary: We have now completed our distribution of all 600,000 ARB so far. As our process developed, the distribution has become increasingly extensive, specific and efficient, with 133 boosts issued in the final period. As of 2 September 2024 (the final date of our incentive period), our LTIPP dashboard shows total TVL at $46m, again far exceeding our total goal for the campaign period around 53%.

phase1_statsChanges: No meaningful changes applied during the first distribution period
phase1_statsLessons: Available incentives are struggling to have an impact against broader market conditions
phase1_actualsSummary: First round of incentives distributed on Monday, June 17th based on bond market activity from Sunday, June 9th through Saturday, June 15th.
phase1_actualsDisclosures: None
phase1_plannedChanges: We followed the distribution plans from our original proposal, distributing 10,000 $ARB tokens across two bond markets capped at 10% of their capacity. We are distributing incentives weekly (Mon) based on transactions from the previous week (Sun-Sat), with the first epoch distributed for June 9th - 15th.
phase1_plannedExpectations: Market conditions are significantly worse than when we initially submitted our LTIPP proposal. As a result, protocols evaluating bond programs have taken a wait-and-see approach. Over the next biweekly period, we will gauge interest in new bond markets and strategize a new approach to pipeline.
phase2_actualsSummary: Bond purchasers have adjusted to the expected incentives and are now reliably purchasing at a premium. The caps implemented at 10% of market volume resulted in fewer incentives distributed than available.
phase2_actualsDisclosures: Second round of incentives distributed 9,785 $ARB on Monday, June 24th for the period from Sunday, June 16th through Saturday, June 22nd.
phase2_plannedChanges: No meaningful changes implemented yet for the second disbursement phase.
phase2_plannedExpectations: Based on the initial under-performance relative to expectations, we are evaluating a reduction in the incentives cap per market.

phase2_statsLessons: We’ve learned that users keep the program a secret to get a higher proportion of each week’s rewards. To counter this, we are introducing a referral program so some of the rewards that were going to new users are reallocated to people who refer new LPs.
phase2_actualsSummary: We’ve noticed uptake isn’t as strong as expected, despite LPs being very excited about their rewards. We’re looking into why more people aren’t participating by interviewing current participants.

phase3_statsLessons: Based on the results, we will promote our program more to increase referrals. We will also audit our referral incentives through Fuul and assess if they're sufficient to bring more unique LPs.
phase3_actualsSummary: We've implemented a referral infrastructure to incentivize LPs to bring new LPs to our ARB incentives program. We will see how this plays out over time.
phase3_plannedChanges: We’ve learned that users are intentionally keeping the program secret from their friends so they get a higher proportion of each week’s rewards. To counter this, we are introducing a referral program so some of the rewards that were going to new users are reallocated to people who refer new LPs.
phase3_plannedExpectations: We expect new referral links to drive virality, an increase in liquidity, and growth of our incentives program.
phase4_statsChanges: We promoted our incentive rewards. We've received constructive feedback from the community for our incentive rewards.
phase4_statsLessons: We will synthesize the feedback from our community members and create a more compelling incentive structure.

phase4_plannedExpectations: We expect new referral links to drive virality, an increase in liquidity, and growth of our incentives program.

phase2_statsLessons:
Retroactively rewarding users who have already deposited funds may build moral among community members (like an airdrop) but it doesn't build new growth and long term success, which is our goal. For that reason I think we'll shy away from Merkl in favor of platforms like Vaultcraft, Layer3 and OKX that only incentivize net new TVL coming through the campaign.
With enough exposure APR rates on a platform come back to Beta around 15%. Therefore on the platforms like Vaultcraft where we still have 188% APR we're increasing our marketing spend to increase awareness and drive TVL toward Beta.

phase6_actualsSummary:
We incentivized Lend ETH through OKX, and Lend USDT through OKX. We increased the incentives on ETH by 250% (to $250K ARB) and decreased the incentives on USDT by 50% (to 50K ARB). There were diminishing returns from OKX at this scale, the TVL did not 2.5x as would happen if the incentives once again returned to beta. The TVL from OKX increased by 75% from $52M to $92M of ETH lent due to these incentives.
We also distributed funds to structured products to eat up the borrowing capacity, however most took 2 weeks to integrate and were only able to start deploying those incentives in the final week of LTIPP.

phase6_plannedExpectations: Each structured product anticipates requiring $8-10M of ETH to borrow. Now that we’ll be incentivizing 4 structured products in total, we should create $32-$40M of ETH borrow pressure, which will hopefully increase lending deposits as well. In anticipation of extra borrow pressure, our increased ETH lending incentivization we anticipate increasing Lent ETH by $30M by doubling our current incentives which based on our previous campaigns should double the TVL before returning to beta.

phase7_statsChanges: We received portions of the LTIPP grant from some of our partners, however in fine tuning the balance in growth of Deposits and Borrows, we maxed out the channels we’d identified to responsibly deploy the incentives. We did give contango 10,000 ARB to deploy in a single day to understand it’s effect. However we ended up returning around 65,000 ARB because we believed it was a better option than spiking APRs right at the end in an unsustainable hoorah.  We also ended up returning 97,000 ARB after LTIPP ended that was returned to us by our partner Cian, who we’d given 100,000 ARB to use as borrowing incentives. Apparently after developing their integration they didn’t have enough time before the 9/2 deadline to ramp up their rewards. We would like to test Cian more in the future, to understand their full potential, now that their integration is built on Arbitrum.
phase7_statsLessons: Compound began LTIPP with the intention of using a native reward contract to distribute rewards. Due to its extended timeline to develop, we found partner structured products built on top of Compound to drive traffic to the underlying protocol, and at it’s height we grew TVL on Compound on Arbitrum to $208M. Most of that TVL came from ETH as the most structured products are built for borrowing ETH against ETH derivatives. We began with small experiments on each platform and in the final weeks doubled down on those making the largest impact. We were able to attract about $260 of TVL for each $1 of incentives distributed through our highest performing partners. In the next LTIPP, we would incentivize consistently through those platforms in a way that gradually increases incentives over time, and then tapers them off to avoid the shock that comes at the end of the LTIPP campaign and retain the most TVL. After LTIPP completed, we retained about 50% of the TVL on our partners, having found tens of millions in capital that was sticky. We’ll do more analysis after some months to understand if the capital moves based on future campaigns we perform and how sticky it truly is. Small experiments this time will help us perform larger sustainable growth in future partnerships with Arbitrum.

phase2_plannedChanges:
Epoch 02: 
ARB++ deposits start June 25th 
Trading starts June 28th 
Trading ends July 05

Incentives will be deployed in the June 25-28 deposit period and withdrawable with deposits at the end of the trading epoch. Funds deposited as ARB remain as ARB being utilized strictly for collateral within the ecosystem. Based on the strategic use of collateral, D2 will engage in further volatility and momentum strategies ontop. This ensures the strategy maintains exposure to ARB, reduces downside volatility, and offers risk-adjusted returns onto. All pnl and incentives will be as ARB at the end of the epoch, effectively simulating single-sided staking type payoff and growth in ARB token volume.

dgnARB strategy launched utilizing ARB++ as a base https://medium.com/@D2.Finance/dgnarb-is-the-one-eyed-man-king-in-the-land-of-the-blind-adfd49b96a64, dgn ETH uses ARB as a strategic asset maintaining an average delta between 20% and 60% of ETH. By leveraging ARB++, dgnARB aims to provide significantly higher exposure to ARB, with delta exposures ranging from 100% to 200%. For example, it can be long 200% ARB while short 100% notional of a basket of other ghost chains.

phase1_statsChanges: No changes were applied during this period
phase1_statsLessons: The speed bonus had a short term effect, but wasn't as effective as our Avalanche incentive program. We don't know whether this is due to our lower visibility on Arbitrum compared to Avalanche or due to the remainder moving to the retention bonus
phase1_actualsSummary: The total eligible TVL hit it's ATH of $9.8mm in the last day. We count this goal achieved.
phase1_actualsDisclosures: This does not take into account the speed / retention bonus, which amounts to 100k ARB over the course of 12 weeks
phase1_plannedChanges: Given some of the expected integrations (Penpie + Uniswap) not being live on DeltaPrime yet in addition to the ARB price drop, we decided on a more aggressive start of the program to get TVL in. For context: the APY is to grow as TVL milestones are reached, So while ~50k out of 250k ARB received seems little, it is (due to us requiring exponentially more ARB as we grow) relatively high
phase1_plannedExpectations: We expect to hit 10mm eligible TVL (borrowed funds deposited in partnerprotocols) by the end of the biweekly period.
phase2_statsChanges: The weekly incentives have been increased in this period to 60.000 ARB
phase2_statsLessons: No lessons learned this period, as all attention went to our TGE
phase2_actualsSummary: Hit 13.7mm eligible TVL. This is in part due to most of the attention going to our token launching.
phase2_actualsDisclosures: On the first of July we had TGE, which is where most of the attention went to.
phase2_plannedChanges: The ARB incentives have increased from 18.140 at the end of the last biweekly to 33.333 due to milestones being hit
phase2_plannedExpectations: According to our internal plan, we expect eligible TVL to increase to 15mm. This is however the final period leading up to our long awaited TGE (1st of july), which we expect take the spotlight from the LTIPP.
phase3_statsChanges: The weekly incentives in this period for PAs has been increased to 80k ARB
phase3_statsLessons: - Despite having a competing incentive program with a larger grant, our Arbitrum TVL did not drop significantly. This is most likely the result of the retention bonus as part of our strategy to keep Arbitrum TVL sticky. Additionally, the TVL is back to growth, indicating the LTIPP program (focused on the Prime Account) catering to a different user-base, despite both parts of DeltaPrime (savings + PA) positively influencing each other regardless of which side is incentivised.
phase3_actualsSummary: Overall our eligible TVL saw a small drop to 12.2mm as funds migrated. This drop has stopped and reverted to positive growth again in the past 3 days.
phase3_actualsDisclosures: In this period Avalanche Boost (an incentiveprogram across a few top Avalanche protocols, including DeltaPrime) went live. While this increased DeltaPrime's overall adoption (as requested in the initial proposal not to opt out of), this did negatively impact our eligible Arbitrum TVL as users who initially bridged to Arbitrum bridged back.
phase3_plannedChanges: No changes will be done in this period
phase3_plannedExpectations: We expect the eligible TVL to surpass 17.5mm
phase4_statsChanges: The weekly PA incentives have been increased to 80k on Monday 22nd.
phase4_statsLessons: The attack itself was a strong signal that we must continue improving our security where possible; the community's response and show of trust (As seen by a continuous increase in TVL post-attack rather than a decrease) taught us that the community can be forgiving if the attack is properly responded to (transparency, clear communication, focus on fund retrieval).
phase4_actualsSummary: In this period our Arbitrum TVL hit its ATH of $26.5M on July 29th. The eligible TVL broke its previous record, but did not hit the next milestone of $17.5M
phase4_actualsDisclosures:
- After 19 months of being live, DeltaPrime experienced its first exploit on the 23rd of July, leading to $1mm being illegally withdrawn from 13 Arbitrum Prime Accounts. Within 48 hours all 13 accounts were made whole, with $900k returned by the hacker and the remaining $100k covered by our POL insurance pool. The full post mortem can be found here: https://medium.com/@Delta_Prime/deltaprime-post-mortem-report-752bd60a25e6
- The TVL on DefiLlama, our dashboard and elsewhere was undervalued. On the first of July the real TVL was $21.875.290 on the 29th of July this was $26.596.603
phase4_plannedChanges: The weekly PA incentives will be increased to 80k if the next milestone is hit
phase4_plannedExpectations: We expect the eligible TVL to move back up to and past its previous level.
phase5_statsChanges: The weekly rate for PAs was lowered to 50k
phase5_statsLessons: No lessons learned in this period
phase5_actualsSummary: Eligible TVL dropped slightly as incentives reduced.
phase5_actualsDisclosures: In this period we did our first sPRIME rev share. Since this was ~50% ROI in a month for holders, a lot of attention came to the token rather than the incentivized pools.
phase5_plannedChanges: The ARB distributed is planned to start reduce as we near the end of the program.
phase5_plannedExpectations: We're expecting to keep our eligible TVL stable as Bonus APR starts slowing down.
phase6_statsChanges: The weekly incentives were reduced to 0 over the course of this period
phase6_statsLessons: While the incentives reduced to 0, the mean TVL over this period reduced with only 12%. This is likely the effect of the retention bonus, rewarding those who keep their liquidity in our partner's protocols past the point where there are weekly incentives.
phase6_actualsSummary: With 2 major black-swan events, the past month has been rough to say the least. Although the community does continuously show their support quantified by the increase in mean TVL, the LTIPP had a backseat to the black swan events. We grew, but not as fast or far as we would have liked, had we been in a position to focus on growth rather than just security.
phase6_actualsDisclosures: On the 13th we became aware of an infiltration by three North Korean IT workers in the DeltaPrime development team. For the next weeks, the main focus went to ensuring code integrity of every line written by these workers. The code was secure, but this did cost time and effort which could have been put to better use.
phase6_plannedChanges: The weekly rate will lower to 0 as we near the end of the program
phase6_plannedExpectations: We expect a portion of users to exit as the incentives get removed. Simultaneously a portion will stay in due to the retention bonus in place.
phase7_statsChanges: During this whole period there were no LTIPP incentives, save for the one-time success & retention bonuses being paid out
phase7_statsLessons: The retention bonus payment led to a large outflow of users. In the future we will keep the retention bonus in, with the difference that it will be paid out over a prolonger period of time after the weekly incentives stop.
phase7_actualsSummary: Nothing new to add here. Overall thoughts were shared in the previous report.
phase7_actualsDisclosures: No disclosures in this period. Weekly incentives have ended, with only Speed + Retention bonuses to be paid out
phase7_plannedChanges: Only the 100k ARB speed and retention bonus will be paid out in this period
phase7_plannedExpectations: Given that the retention bonus will be paid out in this biweekly already, we expect the TVL to drop as the LTIPP fully ends.

grantSummary: Fiat24 is a Swiss regulated financial institution supervised by the Swiss bank regulator. We are likely the first bank that is operating in a truly Web3 way, with all client ledgers booked on public blockchain, and with an NFT used to represent client identification. Holding the Fiat24 NFT in any non-custodial crypto wallet (e.g.: MetaMask) enables clients to operate their account and access their e-banking portal directly from that wallet. In this way, we connect the crypto world to the real world payment network.

phase1_statsChanges: Did not start distribution in this period
phase1_statsLessons: Sync up with foundation well before distribution of any funds as every transaction requires a DAO vote
phase1_actualsSummary: LM started in the next period (June 25th)
phase1_actualsDisclosures: Did not start distributions yet, as we had governance procedures to go through and some small technical stuff to do in order to get everything set up for smooth distributions
phase1_contractsIncentivized: ETH Farming Contract: 0xf3b7994e4dA53E04155057Fd61dc501599d57877; native USDC Farming Contract: 0xd0181a36b0566a8645b7eecff2148ade7ecf2be9
phase1_plannedChanges:
We didn't manage to actually distribute any Arb in this period because we had to do some governance stuff - we previously changed the recipient of the Arb to the foundation multisig (because we have non-contributor anons in our financial multisig and its not fair to ask them to go through the pains of KYC). When foundation then claimed the Arb, the told us we need to do DAO vote for the distribution etc.

We got it all done but the whole process took a bit longer than expected. So in this first period we did not start distributing anything yet.
phase1_plannedExpectations: As of June 25th Arb has gone from foundation multisig -> grants multisig -> financial multisig -> incentive contracts and LM activities have started on Gearbox on the lending side.
phase2_statsChanges: We started distributing incentives in this period (and TVL began to grow rapidly as expected)
phase2_statsLessons: Previously I discussed lessons related to foundation communication and multisig planning, those are the chief takeaways here as we could/should've started a week earlier than we did if all those things were lined up in a row.
phase2_actualsSummary: So far so good, once incentives started TVL got a big boost on the lending side. As we push the borrow side more with various partners + Arb and GEAR incentives, lending side APY should go up even more and TVL should hopefully further increase.
phase2_actualsDisclosures:
We ended up with multiple transactions to move Arb received to the farming contracts.

Foundation received the Arb, but they would have required DAO vote on every single TX so we formed a 'grants' multisig via DAO vote to avoid unnecessary bureaucracy, based on an existing contributors multisig.

From that multisig, we intended to send Arb directly to farming contracts but there was a technical issue. So we ended up needing to send the Arb to the financial multisig before --> the farming contracts.

So the chain of arb is --> foundation multisig --> grants multisig --> financial multisig --> ETH and USDC farming contracts.

We also took a bit longer than expected to start distribution because 1. foundation required votes to send Arb anywhere and 2. We were deploying the native USDC pool to substitute the USDC.e pool which was previously deployed. We did this because Arbitrum contributors and our grants advisors had specified that native USDC over USDC.e was a priority.
phase2_contractsIncentivized: ETH Farming Contract: 0xf3b7994e4dA53E04155057Fd61dc501599d57877; Native USDC Farming Contract: 0xd0181a36b0566a8645b7eecff2148ade7ecf2be9
phase2_plannedChanges: We started distribution to lending pools on 25th of June, midway through this period.
phase2_plannedExpectations: I expect TVL in this period to go up drastically and hopefully to see some demand on the borrow side as there is will now be ample liquidity + we are working to get incentives started on the borrow side as well.
phase3_statsChanges: No changes on the lending side. In this period we started also distributing incentives on the leverage side via STIMMIES (points program) which converts into ARB and GEAR via airdrop.
phase3_statsLessons: I think the initial rollout on STIMMIES has some people confused, we could clarify/simplify this program a bit. As mentioned, while overall TVL is going better than expected and lending supply is there, our utilizations are not as high as we would like (~40-45%). Partially this is because ethena + LRT narratives have quieted down a bit, but ideally we'll still get to ~65% or so so lenders get more organic APYs.
phase3_actualsSummary:
So far so good, we are seeing increasing TVL over time as expected, and pretty much exactly in line with what we had expected in our grant application. We are hoping that utilization will also increase - its not bad right now but could be better. We are running up against limits on the most popular strategies (eth restaking). Increasing limits takes dev time and governance time (these are effectively risk controls, so its good that its not fast, but for the purposes of adjusting to increased demand its not ideal). The STIMMIES program is live as of July 5th, where we're distributing 250k ARB total and 16m GEAR (token amounts, not $$ values). Users can earn points by trading, farming, or via referrals. But as mentioned, some limits are maxed out and increasing them takes time.

TLDR we are doing well on the lending side, TVL is in line or even surpassing expectations at this stage. Utilization ratios could be higher and we are putting the pieces in place to get this going.
phase3_actualsDisclosures: I was at ethCC then went on vacation so I must disclose that I did not submit this on time =(
phase3_plannedChanges: Not much changing,  we are distributing incentives on our lending pools. We are still in the process of implementing the points program that will be the method of distribution of the leverage side.
phase3_plannedExpectations: My expectations for this period were a substantial boost in TVL, in particular on the lending side. Gearbox's brand penetration amongst defi users + the APY from incentives is a strong enough combo to substantially boost TVL.
phase4_statsChanges: This is the first period where incentives on the leverage side (STIMMIES) was running through most of the period. We consequently began to see uptick in utilization.
phase4_statsLessons: Things are broadly going to plan, utilization increased in this period and we were at aveage ~25m in TVL and growing, broadly in line with expectations.
phase4_actualsSummary: TVL is growing at a good pace, we have hit our TVL goals of 30m-35m on Arbitrum with 30m in lending pools. The other side of this is to increase utilization which we are actively working on.
phase4_plannedChanges: No changes. STIMMIES continues, as does the lending side incentives
phase4_plannedExpectations: TVL continues to grow. We are hoping that utilization rates will pick up as well.
phase5_statsChanges:
TVL was at our goal of 30m, then ETH took a dive downwards and brought it back to ~22m from ~30m =(. Vitalik keeps dumping on us.

On the plus side, utilization is up substantially from previous weeks, 70% on ETH and 62% of USDC. We're pretty much in range (or very close to it) for our milestones targets set out in our grant application.

Overall pleased with progress, we were at our TVL target until ETH dropped from ~3300 --> ~2300, which obviously has had a direct effect on our TVL, as well as causing general panic and increased likelihood of withdrawals from protocols/strategies across the board in the defi market. Utilization is up, STIMMIES is working quite well on Arbitrum as well, seems our incentivization efforts are going as planned.
phase5_statsLessons: We've had a couple of slight delays in the distribution of STIMMIES rewards due to confusion in the process to get all the right wallet addresses. A more streamlined and defined process for the processing of STIMMIES would be good, and making the whole process a bit more automated on our end should be a priority, especially if there is a LTIP that goes beyond the "pilot" part.
phase5_actualsSummary: Market tanked so TVL tanked as well, especially since ~70% of TVL was in ETH. On the other hand utilization is up, people are happy farming Renzo and lido with leverage, particularly on the Renzo side thanks to fundamental oracles (which we are introducing for Lido as well shortly). As of the submission of this report, TVL is ~20m but utilization on ETH pool is 74%. USD utilization is still relatively low, we will look at boosting that if we can.
phase5_plannedChanges: No big changes to the plan, just some minor tweaks on which assets are getting extra incentives in STIMMIES (focused on assets that already have some organic demand). e.g with wsteth we are boosting it a bit on the marketing side + giving a 2x STIMMIES multiplier as the LRT popularity has waned a bit and Lido staking yield is starting to look reasonable again in comparison.
phase5_plannedExpectations: We will continue to try to boost utilization, TVL looks reasonable good and rates on that side are good at the moment.
phase6_statsLessons: As the program comes to an end, I think our main takeaway is to try to be administratively and operationally more efficient so that there are no hurdles/roadblocks to enacting our plans re: grant stuff. Risk limits, deployments, and whatnot should all be done more proactively, so that we don't have situations where we have good TVL on the lending side but utilization is underwhelming, not because there is no demand, but because we are up against a risk quota.
phase6_actualsSummary:
As we close out this LTIPP grant, we will attempt to distribute as much of the grants funds to users. We've been a tad slower than we'd like on upping risk/quota/etc limits on popular assets, partially because we have on boarded third party risk curators like Chaos Labs (to be max SAFU), but we're still figuring out the workflow for requesting/suggesting increased limits on our most popular assets/strategies. In the future we will aim to have this workflow optimized and running as smoothly as possible.

Other than that, this period is as expected, pretty good TVL and good utilization.
phase6_plannedChanges: Pretty much carrying on with the plans as stated - some minor changes the rewards for specific assets in STIMMIES, but broadly we are incentivizing LRTs + LSTs + Arb native assets for STIMMIES on the leverage side and incentivizing USDC/ETH pools on the lending side. Less focus on margin trading as it seems people aren't particularly keen to use this feature, would've been a waste of incentives.
phase6_plannedExpectations:
As we come to the close of this program, we'll try to distribute as much of the grant funds as we can to users, potentially some amount will be left over at the end which we'll be happy to send back to Arbitrum foundation (or let the farming continue through September if some extension plan is figured out).

We achieved (more or less) what we set out to, hitting 30m in TVL at some points in the cycle (pre ETH drop from ~3400 to ~2400). Now we're at 23m with good utilization, expect this will continue through the end of the LTIPP.
phase7_statsChanges: no changes
phase7_statsLessons: None really, overall our biggest issues were being a little behind the ball on risk limits, leading to utilization filling up slower than it could have otherwise.
phase7_actualsSummary: Incentives will stream until Sep 2nd then we'll return remaining Arb.
phase7_plannedChanges: None
phase7_plannedExpectations: Last week, things are tapering off, might see a slight TVL decline and whatnot as people exit knowing that incentives are coming to an end.

grantSummary:
ARB tokens will be allocated to increase our liquidity mining incentives in order to attract liquidity providers to the platform. ARB tokens will be allocated to subsidize gas fees by 80% for users who are onboarding to the Arbitrum chains (from any other network). Users exiting Arbitrum One or Nova will not get subsidized. ARB tokens will be allocated to subsidize bonder fees in order to reduce end-user costs to bridge.

Additional Metrics Dashboards can be found here: https://www.openblocklabs.com/app/arbitrum-ltipp/protocols/HOP-Protocol | https://dune.com/hop/hop-arbitrum | https://dune.com/hopteam/hop-protocol-ltipp-arbitrum

phase1_actualsSummary: Due to starting mid-period our usage numbers were lower, however LP incentives are running as expected. We saw about 1,111 ARB used on day per average for the LP Incentives. That is on pace for about 93,000 ARB total during the 12 week period. We expected to see about 1/3rd of our 500k ARB Grant used towards LP Incentives (167k or so), so anticipate as the project gets more underway and we have more data this we will reach our goal.
phase1_actualsDisclosures: Our incentives started on 6/12/2024. There was an update to how USDC is bridged to Arbitrum between the application and launching the incentive program. In short, incentives are no longer needed and as such we have shifted AMM rewards proportionally to other pools. rETH was unable to be incentivized due to technical limitations. MAGIC it was determined due to the effort required to setup the incentivization versus the low usage it was not worth incentivizing over ETH, USDT and DAI.

phase1_statsChanges: In deviation from the original plan (increasing ARB emissions in three phases), we have decided to distribute the same number of ARB tokens every week (linear emissions). Furthermore, we have planned slightly decreasing emissions for the LP rewards over the LTIPP period and slightly increasing emissions for the trader rewards at the same level. The purpose of this is to initially attract enough liquidity that can then serve as a counterparty for traders. The DEX-LP rewards are distributed linearly from weeks 1-12, with the exception that we only distribute half of the rewards for the first 2 weeks and spread the other half over the remaining weeks.
phase1_statsLessons: We have learned that many things can change from the application to be included into the LTIPP, to the state of the market and its effect on sentiment, to the user behavior. We will continue to adapt the incentives as we see the individual activities play out for LP, trading, and DEX liquidity.
phase1_actualsSummary: We are satisfied with the start of the LTIPP program and are confident that we can achieve the first KPIs.
phase1_actualsDisclosures: In deviation from the original plan (increasing ARB emissions in three phases), we have decided to distribute the same number of ARB tokens every week (linear emissions). Furthermore, we have planned slightly decreasing emissions for the LP rewards over the LTIPP period and slightly increasing emissions for the trader rewards at the same level. The purpose of this is to initially attract enough liquidity that can then serve as a counterparty for traders. The DEX-LP rewards are distributed linearly from weeks 1-12, with the exception that we only distribute half of the rewards for the first 2 weeks and spread the other half over the remaining weeks. The metrics have also been adjusted according to the APR expectations set forth in the application and modified for the adjusted ARB price. Discussions have been posted in the governance forum here: https://forum.arbitrum.foundation/t/ipor-protocol-ltipp-application-final/21965/11

The purpose of this is to initially attract enough liquidity that can then serve as a counterparty for traders. The DEX-LP rewards are distributed linearly from weeks 1-12, with the exception that we only distribute half of the rewards for the first 2 weeks and spread the other half over the remaining weeks.
The metrics have also been adjusted according to the APR expectations set forth in the application and modified for the adjusted ARB price. Discussions have been posted in the governance forum here: https://forum.arbitrum.foundation/t/ipor-protocol-ltipp-application-final/21965/11

The trading incentives had the best net positive for the protocol, increasing the real yield generated and the asset on asset returns for LPs.

phase2_actualsSummary:
We received 37500 ARB on June 5th, and started the farming program on June 6th. A total of 20831 ARB was sent to the Masterchef contract which will distribute token rewards to the LP. The rest ARB are retained as grant rewards for projects that'll integrate Integral's liquidity.

Summary
For the period June 17th - July 1st, a total of 19373 out of 20831 ARB has been claimed by LPs. The WETH-ARB pool has reached an astounding 30 d average apr of 346%, not taking into account the additional Arbitrum token incentives.

phase3_statsChanges: The only changes made from the initial application were a slight reorganization of ARB to lessen leaderboard rewards and increase Loot Box/Battle Pass rewards to help onboard more new and unique users instead of rewarding OG users who tend to dominate daily leaderboards.

phase3_actualsSummary: Incentives have been dispersed through the planned verticals, loot boxes, leaderboards, battle pass rewards, and marketplace rebates during the final week of Season 1 of our Play to Rip campaign. This dispersal also includes Season 1 marketplace rebates and Leaderboard rewards. These aforementioned verticals are dispersed only at the end of each of the three seasons.

phase1_statsChanges: Although it's too early to make conclusions the incentives seem to have increased the daily activity.
phase1_statsLessons:
- During the first week, we experimented with various Sybil prevention mechanisms. These included owning a Farcaster account, having a Gitcoin Passport Score of 20+ and owning at least 10 Layer3 CUBEs. 

- We noticed that unreliable Farcaster APIs often resulted in verification fails. As a result, we plan to utilize the Gitcoin passport and Layer3 CUBEs as the preferred mechanism.
phase1_actualsSummary:
- Over the last seven days we launched the Arbitrum Hub campaign including 10 Quests on Layer3. 
- Arbitrum Hub Quests have received ~95,000 participations
- 40,000 ARB have been allocated to individual Quest contracts
- 18,000 ARB have been claimed by users
phase1_actualsDisclosures: We received the first disbursement a week late, on June 10th, which caused a delay in starting the distribution. To make up for lost time, we plan to accelerate the campaign in the upcoming weeks and align with the KPIs and timeline originally outline in the application.

phase2_statsChanges:
- Canceled Clipper Quest due to a malicious Discord Invite in their documentation
- Launched Matcha Quest instead of Aevo
phase2_statsLessons: We deployed 25 Quests, allocating the total 100,000 ARB as planned for the first month. However, some Quests were deployed in the last few days, so the full distribution hasn’t occurred yet. As we close Phase 1 and move to Phase 2, we’ll ensure to launch the Quests earlier to ensure full distribution according to the plan.
phase2_actualsSummary:
Our anti-sybil mechanisms, combining CUBEs and Gitcoin Passport, aim to ensure the highest quality of users receive the Grant. According to our metrics dashboard, we are close to achieving most of our planned metrics for Month 1. Considering the 1-week delay mentioned in the first report, we have been able to catch up on launches and will be able to meet planned targets for Phase 2.
Total Quest launched: 25 Quests
Total ARB allocated: 100,000 ARB
Total ARB distributed: 70,000 ARB
Unique Users: 44,000
phase2_actualsDisclosures:
We launched a Quest featuring Clipper. However, due to a malicious Discord Invite in their documentation, we have removed the Quest to ensure the safety of our users and won’t include Clipper in upcoming Quests.
We changed the initial planned Quest for Aevo to Matcha

phase3_statsChanges: We removed Umami and Savvy from the initial planned Quests. At the end of Phase 1, we allocated 65,000 ARB as rewards for 12,500 users actively engaged in the campaign.
phase3_statsLessons: The gaming sector presents a challenge in finding compatible apps that support the Layer3 traffic. Campaign prizes tend to motivate users to continue Questing and reward those actively engaged.
phase3_actualsSummary: We are observing strong user adoption on the Arbitrum Quests. Additionally, we have allocated an extra 65,000 ARB from our internal budget to provide additional incentives and rewards for power-users participating in the campaign.
phase3_actualsDisclosures:
After reviewing over 30 gaming/NFT apps, selecting a few capable of handling Layer3 traffic is challenging due to the high volume of user activity.

We’ve launched Quests with 0xAstra, Bridgeworld, Treasure, and OpenSea, alongside multiple DeFi-centered apps. We are also planning a coordinated launch with Cosmik Battle.

We had to alter our plans for Umami and Savvy, as QA revealed they couldn’t handle the Layer3 traffic.

phase4_statsChanges: No major changes were made to our campaign strategy. We continued with our planned launches and increased the supply of Quests in the first week of each bi-weekly period. This ensures users have sufficient time to claim their rewards, enhancing overall engagement and reward distribution.
phase4_statsLessons:
We are finalizing the second stage, Iteration, and moving towards Retention. In this phase, our focus will be on targeting users who completed previous Quests and rewarding those with active liquidity on Arbitrum, particularly from a DeFi perspective.

From a gaming perspective, we’ve launched Quests that require users to engage for more than 7 days. This has resulted in good user retention, with many players continuing to participate and requesting more Quests in the gaming sector. This approach is proving effective in maintaining user interest and engagement.
phase4_actualsSummary:
We launched all our planned 25 Quests ahead on the first week of the period, to ensure users had ample time to participate and claim their ARB rewards.

This early launch has been positively received by the community, resulting in a cohort of extremely active and engaged users on the Arbitrum network through these Quests. The proactive approach has enhanced user experience and engagement.

phase1_statsChanges: No Change.

phase1_actualsSummary:
We've launched the LogX $ARB incentives program, achieving an impressive average trading volume of over $70M within just 7 days on the Arbitrum network. 
We introduced the LogX Arbitrum Trading Rush and $ARB fee rebates campaign to incentivize participation on the LogX Arbitrum Network. Additionally, more than 500 participants have tweeted about receiving rebates for depositing funds into their LogX accounts on Arbitrum.
phase1_actualsDisclosures: LogX is an orderbook perpetual DEX that is live on the Arbitrum network. In an orderbook model, TVL is not the correct representation of the DEX's performance. Instead, the right metric to track performance is the total trading volume on the LogX Arbitrum network. We have shared trading volume metrics in the "Finalize" section under the Average Daily TVL field.
phase1_plannedChanges:
There have been some minor changes made to the original proposal to utilize the $ARB grants more effectively. Currently, we are incentivizing users through the following methods:

1. A trading competition worth $15,000 $ARB, based solely on trading volume.
2. $ARB rebates for trading fees worth 5,000 $ARB, available to the first 125 traders. These rebates are distributed weekly on a first-come, first-served basis to all users who tweet about depositing funds on Arbitrum network and verify their transaction. The aim is to reach CEX traders
Both of these events are held on a weekly basis.

Currently, we are not using $ARB for referral and affiliate incentives.
Here's the link of announcement for the same: https://x.com/LogX_trade/status/1800543792988344792
phase1_plannedExpectations: LogX has announced two exciting initiatives on the Arbitrum network: the Arbitrum Trading Rush and a fee rebate campaign. These campaigns aim to incentivize participation on the LogX Arbitrum network by offering rewards from the LogX LTIPP Grant. Our goal is to attract perpetual traders from CEX, other ecosystems, encourage new users to try out LogX on Arbitrum, and drive significant trading volumes.
phase2_statsChanges: We also incentivised our affiliate partners with $ARB tokens to help promote the campaign and bring in a new number of traders
phase2_statsLessons:
1. We've realized that we can significantly improve the distribution of the $ARB campaign to new audiences.
2. Rebates incentivize traders to actively trade throughout the entire week.
phase2_actualsSummary:
We ran three campaigns with the $ARB Grants:

1. Arbitrum Trading Rush Leaderboard: As part of this leaderboard, $15,000 in $ARB tokens and 30,000 $LOGX tokens were distributed to traders.

2. $ARB Trading Fees Rebates: 125 traders received up to 40 $ARB tokens weekly as part of the trading fees rebate program, which is offered on a first-come, first-served basis.

3. We also awarded 5,495 $ARB tokens to our affiliates based on the trading volume they generated on the LogX Platform.
phase2_actualsDisclosures: LogX is an orderbook perpetual DEX that is live on the Arbitrum network. In an orderbook model, TVL does not represent the DEX's performance correctly. Instead, the right metric to track performance is the total trading volume on the LogX Arbitrum network. We have shared trading volume metrics in the "Finalize" section under the Average Daily TVL field.
phase2_plannedChanges: No Change.
phase2_plannedExpectations: Increase in Trading Volume, Acquisition of new users from CEX and retention of existing users.

We ran 3 campaigns with the $ARB Grants 

1. Arbitrum Trading Rush Leaderboard: As part of this leaderboard, $15,000 $ARB tokens and 30,000 $LOGX tokens were given out to traders

2. $ARB Trading fees rebates: 125 Traders received up to 40 $ARB tokens weekly as part of the trading fees rebate program. The program is on a first come first serve basis

3. We also gave out 5495 $ARB tokens to our affiliates based on the amount of trading volume they brought in to LogX Platform
phase3_actualsDisclosures: LogX is an orderbook perpetual DEX that is live on the Arbitrum network. In an orderbook model, TVL is not the correct representation of the DEX's performance. Instead, the right metric to track performance is the total trading volume on the LogX Arbitrum network. We have shared trading volume metrics in the "Finalize" section under the Average Daily TVL field.
phase3_plannedChanges: We have added incentivising our affiliate partners on a custom based deal basis the amount of new traders that come and trade. This will bring us new traders
phase3_plannedExpectations:

1. Increase in volume done on LogX
2. Addition of new traders trading on LogX from Centralised Exchanges

phase1_statsLessons: We had to make minor adjustments to the previously submitted application, emphasizing the need for adaptability when introducing novel concepts like convertible debt within DeFi.
phase1_actualsSummary:
We are pleased to provide an update on the LTIPP program, two weeks into its commencement on 5 June 2024, with the first two-week epoch of the MYSO LTIPP incentive campaign ending on 19 June. Originally, the proposal budgeted 6,000 ARB for rewards, which at the time of the proposal equated to around $12,000 USD. Given the current ARB price now, the incentive budget was adjusted to 11,219 ARB (see also changes on Arbitrum Forum)

To spur initial excitement, we implemented a frontloaded distribution schedule that allocated 11,219 ARB over the first two weeks across all user deposits, with the instantaneous APY applied to users based on their deposit time. This strategy allowed us to generate excitement and attract over $230,000 in user deposits at its peak.

We'll continue our efforts to attract more depositors and funnel users toward loan proposal subscriptions.

This process may not align with the shorter time frame of the 12-week LTIPP program. Nevertheless, we are committed to doing our best to onboard DAO

phase5_statsLessons:
Introducing a new and intricate narrative around convertible debt has been particularly challenging, especially in the current market conditions. Due to the difficulties in engaging DAOs, we've made the decision to discontinue the LTIPP program, as the likelihood of onboarding a DAO within the next few weeks appears slim.

However, we are proud of our achievements on the lender side, where we've successfully attracted lenders, raised awareness about convertible debt, and accumulated significant TVL. We are deeply appreciative of the Arbitrum community for allowing us the opportunity to innovate and reach these milestones.

Milestone 4: Trade $500k in volume to earn 500 $ARB tokens.

phase1_statsLessons:
### Insights from the Bi-Weekly Period

Users have faced challenges in bridging wOETH and prefer depositing native ETH directly into our pools. Our focus will be on encouraging users to bridge more funds from the mainnet.

There was a higher than expected demand for our pool. The next challenge is ensuring this demand translates into growth for our protocol.

phase1_plannedChanges:
### Changes to Incentives Plan

Since the proposal, we have concentrated our incentives on one DEX pool within the Balancer ecosystem, aiming for growth through focused efforts. We plan to continue pursuing money market integrations to provide multiple options for wOETH utilization.
phase1_plannedExpectations:
### Summary of Incentives Plan

Our primary focus has been to build wOETH liquidity by allocating a significant amount of ARB on Aura, which has successfully increased the liquidity for the wOETH-ETH E-CLP pool to over $1 million. Additionally, we are pursuing money market integration to enable users to loop funds on Silo and have allocated incentives to those markets as well.
phase1_plannedContracts: Aura Finance pool 64 rewards stash: 0x96b75f64f2962b539e725a645fc0288726ebe181; Silo multi-sig: 0x80071b39aa896aa12240c5194e42661d671bdfb2; Aura Finance reward distro: 0xca85e2ce206b48ee28a87b0a06f9519abe627451
phase2_statsChanges:
### Changes to Incentives Plan

Instead of spending the intended 10,792 ARB allocated to Silo immediately, we calibrated it until demand was visible as spending the full amount when the liquidity pools were empty wasn’t necessary. Now when we can see clear demand for the market, we will reach the full allocation. We will also increase our allocation to the Gyroscope liquidity pool to grow the DEX TVL to match the demand of the borrowers. 

### Plans for the Next Bi-Weekly Period

We intend to:

- Increase the spend on the Silo market
- Increase the spend on the Gyroscope E-CLP pool.
phase2_statsLessons:
### Insights from the Bi-Weekly Period

People are willing to bridge wOETH from mainnet but it’s a double-edged sword due to the demand from Silo because people on Arbitrum are directly taking the bridged funds that have been supplied to the liquidity pools and taking it to Silo which continuously imbalances the pool which needs more bridging. Although it is a good problem to have that has been manageable so far. 

### Additional Notes and Findings

Clear borrow demand for wOETH which we expect to grow alongside the DEX TVL on Arbitrum.
phase2_actualsSummary: After getting the wOETH-ETH E-CLP pool on Balancer to $1M in liquidity we have been focusing on building out demand for the money market integration which has been successful so far. We have seen $2.5M ETH supplied into the wOETH market on Silo and $1.2M wOETH deposited into the market to be borrowed against. We just finished calibrating this market and we plan on increasing the incentives gradually to match the demand we see for this money market to enable looping of wOETH.
phase2_contractsIncentivized: Aura Finance reward distro: 0xca85e2ce206b48ee28a87b0a06f9519abe627451; Silo multi-sig: 0x80071b39aa896aa12240c5194e42661d671bdfb2
phase2_plannedChanges:
### Changes to Incentives Plan

Instead of spending the intended 10,792 ARB allocated to Silo immediately, we calibrated it until demand was visible as spending the full amount when the liquidity pools were empty wasn’t necessary. Now when we can see clear demand for the market, we will reach the full allocation. We will also increase our allocation to the Gyroscope liquidity pool to grow the DEX TVL to match the demand of the borrowers.
phase2_plannedExpectations: After getting the wOETH-ETH E-CLP pool on Balancer to $1M in liquidity we have been focusing on building out demand for the money market integration which has been successful so far. We have seen $2.5M ETH supplied into the wOETH market on Silo and $1.2M wOETH deposited into the market to be borrowed against. We just finished calibrating this market and we plan on increasing the incentives gradually to match the demand we see for this money market to enable looping of wOETH.
phase2_plannedContracts: Silo multi-sig: 0x80071b39aa896aa12240c5194e42661d671bdfb2; Aura Finance reward distro: 0xca85e2ce206b48ee28a87b0a06f9519abe627451
phase3_statsChanges: The main changes have been to include Dolomite within our incentives plan.
phase3_statsLessons: We saw a TVL drop when the the market dropped but this has now recovered to it’s previous levels and is growing sporadically. Pool APY remains good but still need to get more people to bridge wOETH from Ethereum to Arbitrum as we see when there is high demand for wOETH on Arbitrum the Gyroscope pool gets imbalanced.
phase3_actualsSummary:
### Summary of Incentives Plan

The Silo money market and Gyroscope pool is plugging away while we have been focusing on getting wOETH integrated across different protocols on Arbitrum which is why it can now be used on Dolomite as well which is the latest developments. Thus, we will now also distribute 8000 ARB bi-weekly to Dolomite on top of what we have done with Silo and Gyroscope previously.
phase3_contractsIncentivized: Silo multi-sig: 0x80071b39aa896aa12240c5194e42661d671bdfb2; Aura Finance reward distro: 0xca85e2ce206b48ee28a87b0a06f9519abe627451; Dolomite reward distro: 0xa75c21C5BE284122a87A37a76cc6C4DD3E55a1D4
phase3_plannedChanges:
### Changes to Incentives Plan

The main changes have been to include Dolomite within our incentives plan.
phase3_plannedExpectations: The supply and borrow demand for wOETH on Silo has remained constant throughout the period. It will be interesting to track whether Dolomite follows the same pattern as it could play a big part in our continuous growth as we try to establish ourselves on Arbitrum. We’re still waiting for the Dolomite team to activate incentives on the market before we start to push it through our marketing channels.
phase3_plannedContracts: Silo multi-sig: 0x80071b39aa896aa12240c5194e42661d671bdfb2; Aura Finance reward distro: 0xca85e2ce206b48ee28a87b0a06f9519abe627451; Dolomite reward distro: 0xa75c21C5BE284122a87A37a76cc6C4DD3E55a1D4
phase4_statsChanges:
### Changes to Incentives Plan

Slight increase in Gyroscope incentives from 3663 ARB per week to 5000 ARB per week instead while increasing our bribes on Aura as well. 

We have focused on incentivizing ETH liquidity for our money market on Silo, we will tweak it a bit and test demand for borrowing USDC as well.
phase4_statsLessons:
### Plans for the Next Bi-Weekly Period

We intend to:

- Continue spending on Silo, Dolomite and Gyroscope
- Continue push on the marketing to bring more attention to our pools that have been popular among users.
- Re-calibrate Silo incentives to test USDC borrow demand.

### Insights from the Bi-Weekly Period

Dolomite was very successful and has grown to over $1m TVL over the past week which has been good considering ETH price has fallen significantly over the past week which saw TVL drop across many other pools.
phase4_actualsSummary: Silo and Dolomite incentives have remained constant while we have increased the incentives on Gyroscope slightly as more liquidity is required to facilitate the integrations on other platforms. When our DEX pools grows the TVL directly grows on other platforms as well. And we have seen our TVL double on Silo since the last period which has been pleasing.
phase4_contractsIncentivized: Aura Finance pool 64 rewards stash: 0x96b75f64f2962b539e725a645fc0288726ebe181; Silo multi-sig: 0x80071b39aa896aa12240c5194e42661d671bdfb2; Aura Finance reward distro: 0xca85e2ce206b48ee28a87b0a06f9519abe627451; Dolomite reward distro: 0xa75c21C5BE284122a87A37a76cc6C4DD3E55a1D4
phase4_plannedChanges:
### Changes to Incentives Plan

Slight increase in Gyroscope incentives from 3663 ARB per week to 5000 ARB per week instead while increasing our bribes on Aura as well. 

We have focused on incentivizing ETH liquidity for our money market on Silo, we will tweak it a bit and test demand for borrowing USDC as well.
phase4_plannedExpectations: By looking into other LST related money markets on Silo we noticed that there has been recent demand for USDC lending on top of ETH looping which is why we’re going to start incorporating it into the wOETH money market as well and see if the case would be similar for our pool as well.

phase5_statsChanges: No changes has been made to the incentives plan.
phase5_statsLessons:
### Plans for the Next Bi-Weekly Period

We intend to:

- Continue the spending on Silo, Dolomite, and Gyroscope
- Disregard tweaking Silo incentives to incentivize USDC lending as ETH has been the one that people are the most interested in.

### Insights from the Bi-Weekly Period

The Dolomite and Silo pools have fully rebounded since people de-risked after the sell-off. The Gyroscope pool is lagging a bit but hopefully, it catches up soon. 

### Additional Notes and Findings

More of the same at this stage as the liquidity providers in respective platforms seem to be happy so far.
phase5_actualsSummary: We’ve stuck to the same strategy that have been working so far and choose to not fix something that isn’t broken. We’ve incentivized the silo pool, gyroscope pool and the dolomite pools with the same amounts as previous rounds and will continue to do so most likely until the end of the LTIPP campaign.

phase6_statsLessons:
### Plans for the Next Bi-Weekly Period

This was our last distribution of ARB incentives and we now plan to re-assess and decide how to move forward after the program ends. 

### Insights from the Bi-Weekly Period

There tends to be an increased appetite for leverage as our Dolomite and Silo markets continue to grow at a good rate. 

### Additional Notes and Findings

It is a period of evaluation coming up as we have been happy with how things have gone within the Arbitrum ecosystem.
phase6_actualsSummary: We’ve mostly stuck to the same strategy and but have added DHedge to our distribution to set up a vault on their platform. Apart from that wOETH has continued to grow across other platforms within the Arbitrum ecosystem with over $3m deposited on Silo and over $2m on Dolomite.

phase7_statsChanges:
### Changes to Incentives Plan

No changes as no additional distributions have taken place.
phase7_statsLessons:
### Plans for the Next Bi-Weekly Period

No new plans except for the evaluation of the performance of the LTIPP campaign to determine which integration partners we want to double down on. 

### Insights from the Bi-Weekly Period

There is a demand for LST farming on Arbitrum as using leverage to get higher yield is more efficient on Arbitrum due to the scalability through cheap transactions and the transaction speed. The only bottleneck is that yield tends to become compressed after some time, but when opportunities are available, people are willing to bridge their capital to Arbitrum and capitalize on them. 

### Additional Notes and Findings

Being apart of the LTIPP program has been a great experience and we are grateful for having been given the opportunity.

phase1_statsChanges: N/A - the Average Daily TVL is an estimate of the daily traded volume (we do not have TVL). The Average Daily Transactions is an estimate under we fully build out our subgraph and Dune Dashboard for this.
phase1_statsLessons: Implementing a fee rebate mechanism has led to increased trading volume, especially for a new trading primitive like pair trading. On hindsight, we should have made it easier for users to claim their $ARB AND convert it to $ETH or $USDC directly on our UI rather than having to go to Uniswap or another venue.
