phase1_actualsSummary: Our expectation was to start incentive campaigns and experience an increase in volume, which we absolutely did. The first month milestone was to reach approximately $20 million in total trading volume. We have already launched the first campaign, and it appears we are on track to achieve the entire month's goal halfway through the milestone period!
phase1_actualsDisclosures:
Hi everyone,

Considering the price of ARB almost halved since our grant proposal went live, and our estimations depended on the USD value of ARB back then, after consulting with our LTIPP advisors, we have decided to update the KPIs relative to the price change of ARB.

Here are the modified KPIs:

Phase 1: Launch Trading Challenges (Weeks 1-4)
Milestone: Launch first trading challenges, defining the most fair and productive (stickiness-wise) challenges. Collect information from users and deploy capital on those challenges.
KPIs:

Weekly Trading Volume Goal: Strive to hit a weekly trading volume of ~$5.04M given $33.6K of rewards.
Overall KPI for weeks 1-4: $20.16M
Phase 2: Growth and User Experience (Weeks 5-8)
Milestone 1: Final two weeks of trading challenges (5-6)
Milestone 2: Announce fee rebate plans powered by the advantages of having account abstraction in place for our users. Expect more users and more trades by our users.
Milestone 3: Boosted Referral Program, elevate the power of the grant to boost the rewards a referrer and referee get.
KPIs:

Weekly Trading Volume Goal: Strive to hit a weekly trading volume of ~$4.648M given $16.8K of rewards + proportional expenses for the rebates and referrals.
Overall KPI for weeks 5-8: ~$38.64M in total volume
Phase 3: Growth & Perpie Groups Adoption (Weeks 9-12)
Milestone: Initiate campaigns designed to attract traders to Perpie Groups actively. This will enable them to experience the benefits of sharing, socializing, and following other traders. Keep improving the challenges.
KPIs:

Weekly Trading Volume Goal: Strive to hit a weekly trading volume of ~$4.648M given a continuation of rebates and referrals, and begin a vesting program for reward distribution.
Overall KPI for weeks 9-12: >$56M in total volume
phase1_plannedChanges:
Hi everyone,

Considering the price of ARB almost halved since our grant proposal went live, and our estimations depended on the USD value of ARB back then, after consulting with our LTIPP advisors, we have decided to update the KPIs relative to the price change of ARB.

Here are the modified KPIs:

Phase 1: Launch Trading Challenges (Weeks 1-4)
Milestone: Launch first trading challenges, defining the most fair and productive (stickiness-wise) challenges. Collect information from users and deploy capital on those challenges.
KPIs:

Weekly Trading Volume Goal: Strive to hit a weekly trading volume of ~$5.04M given $33.6K of rewards.
Overall KPI for weeks 1-4: $20.16M
Phase 2: Growth and User Experience (Weeks 5-8)
Milestone 1: Final two weeks of trading challenges (5-6)
Milestone 2: Announce fee rebate plans powered by the advantages of having account abstraction in place for our users. Expect more users and more trades by our users.
Milestone 3: Boosted Referral Program, elevate the power of the grant to boost the rewards a referrer and referee get.
KPIs:

Weekly Trading Volume Goal: Strive to hit a weekly trading volume of ~$4.648M given $16.8K of rewards + proportional expenses for the rebates and referrals.
Overall KPI for weeks 5-8: ~$38.64M in total volume
Phase 3: Growth & Perpie Groups Adoption (Weeks 9-12)
Milestone: Initiate campaigns designed to attract traders to Perpie Groups actively. This will enable them to experience the benefits of sharing, socializing, and following other traders. Keep improving the challenges.
KPIs:

Weekly Trading Volume Goal: Strive to hit a weekly trading volume of ~$4.648M given a continuation of rebates and referrals, and begin a vesting program for reward distribution.
Overall KPI for weeks 9-12: >$56M in total volume
phase1_plannedExpectations: For the next couple of weeks we'd like to achieve a total volume that is higher than the volume we committed to. Since we have already have achieved our first four weeks milestone of $20M of volume our expectation are try and get to $25M-$30M of total volume

phase1_statsChanges: We reduced the amount of rewards allocated to daily competition participants.
phase1_statsLessons:
Over the past two weeks, we have seen that ROI based competitions can be skewed depending on the average collateral used by participants. 
To solve for this, we are launching tiered competitions. In these, there'd be different collateral tiers for traders of different sizes. This way the competition is normalized, letting similar size traders compete with each other in a league. 
Small traders also won't be disincentivised to participate, because they can compete in silver or bronze league. 
Large traders can compete for bigger prizes in diamond and gold leagues, with similar sized traders
phase1_actualsSummary: We had 4 daily competitions (2000 $ARB), a weekly competition (8,700 $ARB), 1 lottery ticket (1,000 $ARB).
phase1_actualsDisclosures: We reduced the amount rewarded to daily participants as daily ROI competitions are easier to game.
phase1_plannedChanges:
We maintained our old incentive structure for daily and weekly competitions. 
We rewarded 8700 $ARB to top 25 weekly winners, 2000 $ARB to 4 daily and 1000 $ARB to lottery.
phase1_plannedExpectations: We expect to see a significant uptick in GMX v2 volume.

phase2_statsLessons: Daily competitions are easy to game due to time constraints on all traders.
phase2_actualsSummary:
We canceled our daily competitions and focused on making on weekly competitions since weekly competitions are harder to game.

3. Silver: 
Min collateral: $1k
Prize Pool: 3k ARB

4. Bronze:

Almost doubled DAU from 15 to 28!
Saw a surge in average daily transactions (53-->132) 
Overall daily volume increased 130K to 148K
phase3_statsLessons:
Main lesson is that our platform is incredibly market dependent. As we heavily rely on trader activity, if OI gets wiped and they are hit hard by a sudden dip/liquidation, there's typically a lag time for them to get back in the market as sentiment is poor. Thus our metrics suffer. 

Aside from this, we need to continue shouting from the rooftops about the $ARB incentives now that we've finally started dishing them out. Showing the amount some users are receiving currently should be incentive enough for folks to try our platform out and attempt to qualify.

phase3_statsChanges: Big increase from prior bi-weekly period
phase3_statsLessons: ARB betting tournament was a huge success driving users and betting volume; SX Bet did $350,000 in betting volume on the final day of the betting tournament (a multiple of all other Arbitrum gamblefi dapps). Email offers + targeted outreach to existing SX community spurred engagement and metrics across the board. We learned that we should go really big on the betting tournament in August rather than trying to do many small promos. Also - partnerships with other Arbitrum protocols allows us to multiple the rewards to our community - the three-way trading tournament with Steer and Sushiswap is a testament to this.
phase3_actualsSummary: Total of 81,194 ARB spent between the first betting tournament and email offers to SX power users.
phase3_actualsDisclosures:
69,433 ARB spent in the SX Summer Kickoff Betting tournament
11,761 ARB spent in targeted email marketing offers to KYC'ed SX whale bettors
phase3_plannedChanges:
The $69,420 SX Summer Kickoff betting tournament concluded on July 14th, with ARB disbursements going out the following day, as originally planned this as done in Month 2 of the Arbitrum grant. Only big change as of now - we haven't yet implemented the referral mechanic for getting new users; instead we've supplemented the tournament with some targeted email offers to existing SX bettors that are KYC'ed to motivate them to utilize SX on Arbitrum. This has been small scale - only 11,761 ARB spent on them. 

We have changed slightly from the original proposal regarding the liquidity mining mechanic - rather than LP rewards, we are launching a trading tournament in conjunction with Sushiswap and Steer protocol to promote trading volume; Sushi and Steer are putting up some of their ARB rewards for LP incentives (negating the point of us doing so). This trading tournament will end on July 25th, with 40,000 ARB up for rewards.
phase3_plannedExpectations: We expect betting volume to tail off a little bit as there's a break in the following bi-weekly period before the next betting tournament (planned to start on July 26th). We are experimenting with some targeted email marketing offers in the meantime before the full launch of the referral program end of July. This is effectively the calm before the storm (in August). SX is now the largest gamblefi dapp on Arbitrum by betting volume, this upcoming period is the experimentation phase before SX becomes the largest gamblefi dapp in all of web3 in August.
phase4_statsLessons: Learned a lot from the Sushi trading tournament, primarily on the last day when engagement on the tournament was highest. Main thing we learned is that we should break up the trading tournaments into a series of 3-day or 1-day micro tournaments with smaller prize pools versus the one big tournament. That way we could engagement up across the tournament as users wouldn't be super far behind or super far ahead. Additionally, it would make the tournaments more fun as prizes would be getting paid out throughout versus all at the end. Email offers continue to work but they aren't highly scalable if limited to big bettors so we need to broaden out them.
phase4_actualsSummary: Total of 46,618 ARB spent across trading tournament and email offers
phase4_actualsDisclosures:
40,000 ARB spent on Sushi Trading tournament

phase4_plannedChanges:
Instead of 60,000 ARB for LP incentives as originally proposed, we're instead spending 40,000 ARB for a trading tournament in a three-way partnership with SushiSwap and Steer protocol where they are providing their own ARB for an incentivized LP pool and trading tournament to boost liquidity and trading volume for a SX/WETH pair on Sushiswap. This trading tournament ends on July 25th.

We are also planning on spending 20,000 ARB on referral program (as originally planned) and 20,000 ARB on targeted email offers, given the success they've had recently in prior weeks.
phase4_plannedExpectations: We expect to continue to see growth across all our major metrics, with a particular focus on betting volume. We're a few days away from hitting $5m of cumulative betting since launch; our goal for the following 2 weeks is to hit $7.5m in cumulative betting volume (an average of $200,000 of bets/day) over the following 2 weeks. We'd also like to see trading volume on the SX/WETH token hit a high of $100,000/day on Arbitrum now that the trading tournament is being disbursed.

1. Minting an NFT, registering, and activating SafePal banking gateway: Increased to 20 ARB per person
2. Topping up at least 10 USDC: Increased to 10 ARB per person

phase3_statsChanges: There were no changes from the plan, we ran into some issues with rewards distribution through merkl that led to some delays, but we were able to work with their team to work through this to deploy LP incentives.
phase3_statsLessons: Incentivized stable pools saw the greatest growth during this period. While speculative, this may be driven by users migrating funds to stable assets during times of higher volatility or when downwards price movement is anticipated or occurring. We had some users report confusion around APRs of the $ARB boosted pools - we have reported to Merkl and hope to work towards a resolution to ensure users feel confident that the numbers displayed align closely with what they can earn.
phase3_actualsSummary: The last couple of weeks saw a decline in asset prices along with some drop in total volume. Similarly, Steer Protocol has seen a decline in the US dollar value of assets managed. Overall, we have maintained our TVL well above where it was at the start of the LTIP program -- showcasing the impact of incentives on capital flight during periods of heightened volatility or declining asset prices.

phase4_statsChanges: No meaningful changes made by Steer during this phase.
phase4_statsLessons: Some assets may not have sufficient liquidity to meet the needs of traders - these present a strategic opportunity to deploy incentives to drive LPs to participate and build liquidity for healthier markets. Driving LPs to pools with high volume and higher fee tiers allows for LPs and Steer Protocol to benefit from the high trading volume, and allows traders to have a smooth experience with trading these asset pairs.
phase4_actualsSummary: Stable pools, followed by bluechip/stable pairs have continued to be the greatest drivers of increases in TVL. Large cap assets pairs like ZRO/WETH that have seen significant amounts of trading volume, requiring deeper liquidity to ensure positive experience for traders and in turn benefitting the most from concentrated and automated active liquidity management -- the increased volume of trading has driven significant fees for users that LP into these pools.

grantSummary: Synonym Finance received a 400K ARB grant to kickstart increased lending activity on its next-generation multi-chain lending markets. The goal of the grant was to draw in users from other chains and incentivize to make Arbitrum their home for DeFi, regardless of what other chains they may be interested in.

phase2_statsLessons: No meaningful changes in this period - this was the last period prior to our SYNO Summer kickoff.

phase2_actualsDisclosures: SYNO Summer initial release - will flow into smaller weekly distributions after.
phase2_plannedChanges: We'll be releasing our first tranche of BIPS Season One rewards on June 26th. We plan on releasing rewards on a weekly basis that will be tied to the guide we're releasing on June 24th.
phase2_plannedExpectations: We expect to release 120K ARB to kick off SYNO Summer.
phase3_statsChanges: Our TVL has nearly tripled to date with significant growth in both ARB collateral usage and deployment of stablecoins on the Arbitrum network.
phase3_statsLessons: We've learned that stablecoin loan originations are highly incentivized by the combination of attractive incentives rates and the availability of collateral assets like ARB.
phase3_actualsSummary: During this phase we have kicked off SYNO Summer. We used a large chunk of our initial deposits to reward users who have been depositing on Synonym and making Arbitrum their home base, even if they are interested in other chains (true stickiness).
phase3_actualsDisclosures: We used the majority of our incentives during this period to kick off our SYNO Summer rewards program.
phase3_plannedChanges: SYNO Summer rewards campaign, fuelled mainly by our LTIPP rewards, just kicked off! We expect to see significant growth this period forward.
phase3_plannedExpectations: We're expecting significant TVL growth across our incentivized Arbitrum markets, especially ARB and USDC.
phase4_statsChanges: Meaningful increases in protocol and Arbitrum TVL.
phase4_statsLessons: USDC remains the most popular LTIPP-incentivized market.
phase4_actualsSummary: SYNO Summer x LTIPP Campaign ongoing during this period. No additional disclosures.
phase4_actualsDisclosures: SYNO Summer x LTIPP Campaign ongoing during this period. No additional disclosures.
phase4_plannedChanges: We officially started our SYNO Summer incentives program during this period. We started with the largest rewards and will be balancing them downward over time. The start of this program, which is mainly fuelled by LTIPP, has nearly tripled our TVL in less than 2 weeks.
phase4_plannedExpectations: We expect to see continued growth in Arbitrum stablecoin loan origination and increased utilization of ARB as collateral.
phase5_statsChanges: No meaningful changes, growth continuing in key markets.
phase5_statsLessons: As soon as markets become heavily incentivized, users demand a wider variety of collateral (mainly ETH derivatives) almost immediately.
phase5_actualsSummary: Grant program is moving forward well. We're fortunate to have been able to combine W (Wormhole) emissions with ARB to make a large impact. Users demanding more Arbitrum LSTs.
phase5_plannedChanges: SYNO Summer continued through this period with the addition of multiple new assets, including new ones on Arbitrum, that have driven more attention to our LTIPP Program. We have seen steady growth that has started to taper off after our initial push, but Arbitrum markets are still growing.
phase5_plannedExpectations: We expect continued growth in our primary Arbitrum USDC market as well as more interest in using Arbitrum LRTs/LSTs as prime collateral. Many Arbitrum-native users have requested this and we have delivered on their requests.
phase6_statsLessons: Learned that TVL will adjust downward even within an emissions period as users start to plan their next earning strategy (ex shifting to STIP LP options).
phase6_actualsSummary: This period was marked by a slowdown in deposit growth and no net-new Arbitrum asset additions.
phase6_plannedChanges: No net new ARB asset additions in this period. Maintained ARB emissions rate.
phase6_plannedExpectations: We expect a general slowdown as emission eligibility draws to a close for LTIPP.
phase7_statsChanges: Expected decline in money market activity occurred as incentives came to an end. We did see some more sticky liquidity remaining in our ARB USDC markets however.
phase7_statsLessons: In future rounds of incentives, we need to create more effective retention mechanisms that can delay the flow of incentives to users with stickier liq/activity.
phase7_actualsSummary: SYNO Summer (LTIPP period) ended in this stage.
phase7_actualsDisclosures: SYNO Summer (LTIPP period) ended in this stage.
phase7_plannedChanges: Our TVL dropped significantly as ARB incentives came to a close in this period.
phase7_plannedExpectations: We expected this drop as farmers/LPs seek new opportunities or the extended eligibility of STIP through to late September.

grantSummary:
Threshold DAO will launch a “Bitcoin on Arbitrum Season” campaign with the launch of grant incentives deployment. The campaign will have 4 parts :

- Liquidity incentives with the grant for liquidity providers and tBTC users 
- Incentives for tBTC use on Lending Protocols
- Direct tBTC Minting on Arbitrum Dashboard: Bitcoin to Arbitrum
- Long term incentives : A loyalty program to incentivize both long-term participation and substantial liquidity contribution

phase1_plannedChanges:
For this program, our methodology begins with a heuristic targeting underrepresented pools on Arbitrum (e.g., disproportionately low volume vs. other chains), aiming to increase overall TVL on the chain, differing from our previous approach that focused on capturing market share from competing DEXs. Once the pools are identified and incentives initialized, we will transition to a model-driven approach for subsequent allocations. The 30 pools you see in the first batch of recs were ones we identified as underserved, as well having filtered out some tokens manually after additional DD.

While the previous Arbitrum LM campaign relied on a simulation-based framework to identify pools with greatest boosts to price execution under different liquidity scenarios, this program employs a predictive model that reallocates a fixed budget towards pools with the highest response to incentives, ensuring dynamic and efficient allocation. This method maximizes TVL growth by directing resources to pools that show the most significant growth per dollar of incentives. We'll also consider adding and removing pools as the program goes on if some pools are unresponsive to incentives - this is something we've observed in the past and quickly respond to maintain incentive spend efficiency.

Our public dashboard, with all contracts being incentivized with labels can be found here: 
https://app.hex.tech/a22e666c-4bc7-40d3-93ed-45a25554a14e/app/975a9c84-44a3-4269-8842-33fdb7377fa7/latest
phase1_plannedExpectations: Gauntlet will continue to monitor the performance of these incentives to ensure maximum efficiency and positive impact. Pools may be added, removed, or adjusted. Every 2 weeks will report on the progress of the program and any incentive allocation changes, all of which will be visible on the dashboard linked above.
phase2_statsChanges:
The new incentive allocations were based on an internal model we've developed which measures each pool's elasticity and responsiveness to incentives. The re-allocation favors pool which yield the highest TVL growth for each $1 of incentives. Given this, we expect TVL to continue to increase.
In order to prevent any drastic thrash for LPs which could result in LP exiting,, the allocation changes were capped at 20% and rebalanced to a fixed 150k ARB tranche. As the program develops, the new allocations will converge to the model's true allocations for max efficiency.
phase2_statsLessons: As expected, TVL immediately jumps upon incentives being implemented, along with the counts of LP aiming to take advantage of incentives. However, its important to assess for any meaningful changes in marketshare. In the upcoming weeks we will monitor how newly distributed rewards impacts volume and market share so as to control for market and price fluctuations. Importantly, we wanna ensure that a disproportionate amount of volume isn't being driven by LP rebalances or any predatory JITL behaviour.

phase3_statsLessons:
here has been a decrease of 11.6% in TVL over the past two weeks, when compared to the previous 2-week period. On the other hand, the average daily transactions increased by 11.8% compared to the previous 2-week period, and the average daily unique users increased by 21.2%
The pools that showed the highest elasticity of TVL with respect to incentives were USDC-USDT 0.01%, LINK-WETH 0.3%, and WETH-wstETH 0.01%, while the pools that showed the lowest elasticity were ENS-WETH 0.3%, UNI-USDC 0.3%, and RNDR-WETH 1%. phase3_actualsSummary: During the previous week, the incentive allocation across different pools was reorganized based on expectations about the impact of incentives on the TVL of each pool. The amount of incentives allocated to WETH-wstETH 0.01%  and USDC-USDT 0.01% were reduced the most, and USDe-USDT 0.05% received the largest increase in allocation.
phase3_plannedChanges:
TVL had seen a dramatic increase immediately once incentives were introduced, however it looks like in recent days this has been pulling back, primarily driven by the WETH-wstETH 0.01% pool. Still, TVL is approximately 24% higher than 2 weeks prior to incentives first landing.
Average daily volume also has shown a trend reversal once incentives were introduced but would be more useful to view from a marketshare perspective, which we can include in the next update.
Number of users (LPs) has shown a dramatic step up (almost doubling, with an average of ~39 LPs across all pools to about 73 to Jun 29), with another significant step-up occurring on Jun 30 (+9% increase).
Next we plan on including marketshare metrics in our monitoring so as to normalize against any market effects.
phase3_plannedExpectations: Our analysis predicts further positive impact in TVL and LP count due to the high elasticity of rewards. As the program matures, it can be expected that volume and market share metrics will start to respond stronger as their response to the rewards usually takes longer.
phase4_statsChanges:
As mentioned in the previous section, the pools with the highest absolute changes in TVL over the past two weeks have been WETH-wstETH 0.01% and USDC-USDT 0.01%. It may come as a surprise, then, that these are also the pools whose incentives we recommend reducing by the largest amount.
The underlying driver for this decision is that these pools all started off with some of the largest TVLs as of 07-05, and the changes represent low percentage changes relative to baseline. We fully expect these pools to continue to show TVL growth in terms of absolute dollars, but that is not where we believe we are getting the biggest bang for our buck.
Instead, the pool that would see the biggest increase in incentives under our recommendations is USDe-USDT 0.05% - another pool with relatively high TVL, though not as much as the two previously mentioned.
phase4_statsLessons:
Over the past two weeks, all but two pools showed an absolute increase in TVL. The pools with the highest absolute changes in TVL have been high-cap pools such as WETH-wstETH 0.01%, USDC-USDT 0.01%, and USDe-USDT 0.05%. This increase in TVL came in spite of the fact that we reduced our incentives to these pools by a large amount in the previous round. This suggests that these pools are relatively inelastic with respect to incentives.
USDe-USDT 0.05% was also one of the pools with the highest percentage changes in TVL relative to its baseline. This pool showed a 3.1% increase in TVL on top of an already large baseline, suggesting that there is a strong potential for growth there. The pools that showed the highest percentage increase, however, were the low-cap USDC-XSGD 0.05%, which showed nearly a 9% increase in TVL relative to 07-05, followed by MATIC-WETH 0.3%, which showed a 5.6% increase.

The pools that showed the highest elasticity of TVL with respect to incentives were USDC-USDT 0.01%, LINK-WETH 0.3%, and WETH-wstETH 0.01%, while the pools that showed the lowest elasticity were ENS-WETH 0.3%, UNI-USDC 0.3%, and RNDR-WETH 1%. However, the difference in elasticity was not very large, and the largest driver of the change in TVL across pools was the pool size - pools with a lower TVL showed a greater impact from incentives.
phase4_plannedExpectations: Over the past two weeks, high-cap pools such as USDC-USDT 0.01%, LINK-WETH 0.3%, and WETH-wstETH 0.01% were the most responsive to incentives, while low-cap pools such as ENS-WETH 0.3%, MATIC-WETH 0.3% USDC-XSGD 0.05, and RNDR-WETH 1% were the least responsive. However, apart from these pools, there was not a great deal of difference in elasticity across other pools, and the largest driver of the change in TVL across pools was the pool size - pools that have a lower TVL to start with showed a greater impact from incentives. Based on these patterns, we have allocated more incentives to pools that have moderate TVLs (showing some potential to grow), and also showed higher elasticity in the past weeks (showing a greater impact from incentives).
phase5_statsChanges: No meaningful changes or adjustments over the 2 week period
phase5_statsLessons:
The past two weeks, overall TVL grew considerably, even though six pools showed an absolute decrease in TVL. This decrease was likely an early sign of the market correction that occurred at the beginning of the week of August 5th. The actual impact of the correction on pool TVL will only be known with certainty at the end of the current round of incentives.
The two pools with the highest TVL going into the beginning of the period also showed the highest increase in absolute TVL over the period - these were USDC-USDT 0.01% and WETH-wstETH 0.01%. These were also the pools that received the largest decrease in incentives in the round 4, relative to round 3. As before, these results suggest that removing incentives from these large pools does not have a significant impact on TVL growth, and so incentives should be reallocated away from these pools and towards pools where they would be more effective.
On the other hand, pools with the largest percentage increase in TVL were relatively smaller pools such as MATIC-WETH 0.3%, ENS-WETH 0.3%, and USDC-XSGD 0.05%.

The following observations relate to the week from Jul 29 through Aug 4, so does not include observations on the drop in TVL due to the market downturn that occurred in the early days of this week. Observations on the downturn will be included in our end of period report that will be delivered next week. At the same time, some decrease in TVL was observed by Aug 4.

The pool to which the most incentives were allocated in the last round was USDe-USDT 0.05%. In the past week, TVL in this pool has risen by almost 200%. On the other side, the pools WETH-wstETH 0.01% and USDC-USDT 0.01% had the biggest decrease in incentives relative to the previous week. This led to TVL decreases of 14% and 9% respectively in those two pools. The decrease is not surprising, but the key point to note is that our weekly reallocations cause smaller decreases in the TVLs of pools that lose incentives and bigger increases in the TVLs of pools that gain incentives. Additionally, the two pools that lost incentives continue to remain the largest two pools, suggesting that continuing to incentivize them would be wasteful.

phase6_statsChanges:
The pools that showed the biggest changes in TVL relative to allocated incentives were the largest ones, and that was reflected in the share of incentives allocated to those pools in the current round. For example, pools like WETH-wstETH 0.01%, USDC-USDT 0.01%, and USDe-USDT 0.05% were allocated a moderate amount of incentives, in spite of having high TVLs, which, as we have mentioned in earlier updates, tends to reduce the impact of incentives.
However, the pools that received the largest increases in incentives relative to the previous round were mid-sized pools such as MATIC-WETH 0.3%, FRAX-USDT 0.05%, and USDC-XSGD 0.05%. These pools showed reasonably high impact from incentives, while also getting a boost from their relatively smaller size.
phase6_statsLessons:
The most significant event that occurred during the past two weeks was the market drop on August 5. On that day, TVL declined from $33.9M to $25.9M – a decline of almost 25%. However, TVL had recovered to pre-drop levels on August 9, following which, it even exceeded the earlier peak, before stabilizing at around $33M.
This trend was also reflected in the largest pools. For example, the TVL of WETH-wstETH 0.01% and USDC-USDT 0.01% declined by 30% and 28% on August 5, respectively, and then increased by 37% and 22% over the next 4 days.
These rapid recoveries are evidence of high elasticity, and these large pools were the ones that showed the most impact from incentives over the period.

phase6_plannedChanges:
Most of the pools with the largest decreases in incentives in the current round (Round 5) of recommendations are pools with high TVL, whose incentives had already been reduced in previous rounds, but which continued to show positive growth in spite of these reductions. Of the 5 pools with the largest decrease in incentives relative to the previous round, 4 are large and growing pools rETH-WETH 0.05%, eUSD-USDC 0.05%, USDC-USDT 0.01%, and WETH-wstETH 0.01%. These pools comprise of established tokens and stablecoins, which makes us believe that they will continue to attract TVL even with fewer incentives.
At the same time, the pool with the largest reduction in incentives relative to the previous round is USDe-USDT 0.05%, which also showed a considerable increase in absolute TVL, but was also one that had received a very large increase in incentives during Round 4. Now that it has entered the group of pools with high TVL, we believe that we can safely reduce the incentives allocated to it, and let the flywheel effect take over.
phase6_plannedExpectations: At the end of this phase, we expect to gather data both immediately after post program end and 30 days after to prepare a full program retro report. This report will measure impact on program KPIs and identify insight for future ecosystem growth, similar to our previous program retro linked here: https://www.gauntlet.xyz/resources/results-and-analysis-arbitrum-liquidity-mining-program
phase7_statsChanges: No meaningful changes were made during the 2 week period
phase7_statsLessons:
The story over the past two weeks can be divided into two periods: First, there was a decline in overall TVL as well as in most pools as the market corrected itself after the recovery that we saw in the previous two weeks. Following that, we observed small increases as TVL settled at a stable level just as the incentive program drew to a close.

Overall, TVL declined by 12% between August 25 and September 8, with the decline taking place almost entirely during the week of August 25-September 1. At the end of program, total TVL stood at just under $30M, compared to approximately $15M before the program began.

In our most recent set of recommendations, we recommended increasing the incentives to certain mid-cap pools, such as USDC-XSGD 0.05% and FRAX-USDT 0.05%. These two pools showed positive growth over both weeks in the last period, with USDC-XSGD 0.05% showing an increase in TVL from $159k on August 25 to $369k on September 8 - more than a doubling in TVL.

On the other side, while most pools showed a decline in TVL over the past two weeks, they declined less in many of the pools whose incentives were decreased in the past two weeks. For example, the pool USDC-USDT 0.01%, which is the second largest in terms of total TVL received the second largest decrease in incentives, but its TVL only declined by 4%, which was well below the 12% decline across all pools.

Since the final tranche of incentives has been distributed, there are no further recommended changes. We will provide a retrospective summary of the program after we have been able to observe the changes to metrics in the four weeks after the end the of the incentive program.
phase7_actualsSummary: Full results will be posted for the final 2 week report
phase7_actualsDisclosures: No further disclosures at this time, all funds were utilized.
phase7_plannedChanges:
As mentioned in the previous section, the pools that showed the biggest changes in TVL relative to allocated incentives were the largest ones, and that was reflected in the share of incentives allocated to those pools in the current round. For example, pools like WETH-wstETH 0.01%, USDC-USDT 0.01%, and USDe-USDT 0.05% were allocated a moderate amount of incentives, in spite of having high TVLs, which, as we have mentioned in earlier updates, tends to reduce the impact of incentives.


However, the pools that received the largest increases in incentives relative to the previous round were mid-sized pools such as MATIC-WETH 0.3%, FRAX-USDT 0.05%, and USDC-XSGD 0.05%. These pools showed reasonably high impact from incentives, while also getting a boost from their relatively smaller size.
phase7_plannedExpectations: At the end of this phase, we expect to gather data both immediately after post program end and 30 days after to prepare a full program retro report. This report will measure impact on program KPIs and identify insight for future ecosystem growth, similar to our previous program retro linked here: https://www.gauntlet.xyz/resources/results-and-analysis-arbitrum-liquidity-mining-program

grantSummary:
Vaultka's LTIPP distributes ARB rewards to both vaults and lending pool depositors.

For vault users, a portion of the ARB allocation will be provided as one-time incentives. New users can earn these incentives by depositing into our vaults above the minimum threshold. The incentives will be distributed bi-weekly and can be claimed on our airdrop page.

In addition to attracting new users, a portion of ARB has been reserved to provide one-time incentives to attract new and existing lenders and to encourage depositors to make new deposits in our lending pools. This will open up more leverage opportunities for our vault users.

Furthermore, a portion of the ARB will be allocated as rewards to our existing vaults to maintain our users and allow them to earn a share of the yield. Each vault has a base allocation, and the allocation will change according to market demand.

Finally, ARB rewards have been allocated to USDC lending directly, anticipating the change in USDC usage and the potential increase in demand for the USDC pool. Users can earn ARB rewards by depositing into the USDC pool directly and claim ARB rewards bi-weekly.

The ARB reward distribution will be subject to adjustment bi-weekly to adapt the market conditions

phase1_plannedChanges:
The First bi-week ARB distribution follows the overall proposed structure from the proposal, with a 65%:35% distribution between lending and strategic vaults. Here's a more detailed plan for the first bi-week:

1. Incentives to Attract New Depositors: 46% of the grant is reserved for one-off incentives to new users:

- 6% is allocated to boost new deposits into vaults. Each new deposit will share a pool of 6% of ARB tokens or receive 100 ARB, whichever is lower, as a bi-weekly one-off incentive. We've set a minimum deposit threshold to prevent sybil attacks, making it less profitable to exploit by using multiple wallets. We'll monitor addresses closely and adjust policies bi-weekly as needed.

- 40% is targeted at incentivizing new deposits from lenders:
        - 24% will be used to reward new addresses making new deposits bi-weekly with a one-off incentive. A minimum deposit threshold will be enforced here as well, discouraging the creation of multiple wallets as it may not be profitable.
        - 16% is dedicated to encouraging existing users to make new deposits into lending pools. Similar to the above, new deposits can claim a one-off bi-weekly incentive, amounting to either a share of the pool or 100 ARB, whichever is lower.

2. Maintaining Existing Depositors:

- 30% will go to existing vaults. We're implementing a tier system to ensure that smaller vaults also receive a fair share of the yield, promoting a balanced and diverse ecosystem. The allocation is based on:
•    A base of 4000 ARB allocated bi-weekly for each vault.
•    An additional 4000 ARB for vaults with TVL exceeding $2 million.
•    We're particularly focused on promoting the GM Delta-Neutral, given that alt-coin lending pools are underutilized. Increasing borrowings here could significantly boost revenue for alt-coin lenders.
Example allocations:
•    GLM: 4000 ARB
•    GM Delta Neutral: 18000 ARB
•    GM Leverage: 8000 ARB
•    VLP: 8000 ARB
•    HLP: 8000 ARB
•    KLP: 4000 ARB

- 24% will be directed to USDC lending pool, particularly as we anticipate a shift to USDC usage due to changes from Vela. With the upcoming switch in VLP to USDC and expected freeing up of USDC.e liquidity, there will be an increased demand for the USDC pool.
phase1_plannedExpectations: Expect to bring in around 500 new users to our lending and vaults, with the extra incentives for new users.
phase2_statsChanges: The utilization rate cap of lending pools are loosened, to allow more users to close and reopen their positions before the force closing date.
phase2_statsLessons: The community's response to the newly introduced Maturity Date feature has been positive, even in the face of a modest reduction in TVL. Our priority remains steadfastly on the long-term health and development of Vaultka, rather than on transient fluctuations.
phase2_actualsSummary: Together with the arrangement on introducing the maturity date feature, the LTIPP successfully attracted more transactions and dau, with users closing and reopening their positions. This brings up the revenue of the protocol with higher turnover rate of positions, transitioning to higher redistribution of yield to VKA token stakers.
phase2_plannedChanges:
Together with the launch of the "Maturity Date" feature of Vaultka, the emission for LTIPP this week will be focusing on encouraging redeposits of strategic vaults positions. To incentivize such action, following will be done:

- All new positions opened in vaults will receive a one-off incentive with a minimum threshold in place to prevent Sybil attacks and ensure fair distribution.
- This incentive is designed to promote active use of vaults during this transition, ensuring a seamless shift.
- Incentives for Lending pools will be maintained to keep the lending APR robust and attractive, to continue grow our lending liquidity
- With old positions closing and new ones opening under the new terms, expect higher turnover rates and enhanced lending capital efficiency.
phase2_plannedExpectations: A smooth transition in migrating users, to redeposit and reopen new positions with the new Vaultka feature - Maturity Date.
phase3_statsChanges: The Maturity date feature has launched, and positions that are not closed by users itself, is forced to close during the week of 8th July. Users funds are sent back to their wallet. Most users have reopened their positions with the returned funds, keeping the utilization rate of lending pools high.
phase3_statsLessons: Tier-based and one off incentives for new deposits have been showing effective to attract new capital and users. There is a inflow of new users, together with some larger deposit size, showing that Vaultka's lending pool has remain its attractiveness.
phase3_actualsSummary:
Saw a positive inflow of deposits onto our USDC lending pools, with a few large deposits, showing that our incentives might be effective to reach and attract larger depositors and users. The USDC lending pool TVL grew ~$1mil over the two weeks, with a smooth transition throughout the force-close of positions during the week of 8th July.

There is a rise of DAU, and fees, due to the close of positions with the maturity date feature introduced, generating more revenue for the protocol, and its token holders.
phase3_plannedChanges:
3rd Bi-week distribution will have the same aim of boosting usage and TVL for vaults and lending pools. 

We're introducing a tier-weighted system for ARB allocations that spans both lending pools and vaults. This system is designed to encourage depositing and maintaining larger deposits. A minimum threshold for the lowest tier ensures that splitting assets across multiple wallets won't be beneficial. 
We've also noticed a surge in demand for USDC.e lending pools, so we're rolling out one-off incentives to fuel new deposits into these pools. Measures are in place to safeguard against Sybil attacks, promoting fair and substantial contributions.
phase3_plannedExpectations: This approach will balance and enhance activity across more vaults beyond just the GMX vaults, such as the HMX vault, which has also demonstrated high demand. Our goal is to stabilize or increase TVL in the short term despite recent market downturns.
phase4_statsChanges: No changees applied during the biweek period.
phase4_statsLessons: The USDC lending pool TVL is steadily growing, with a utilization rate consistently around 90%. This balance leaves room for demand to grow while allowing lenders to withdraw freely. The tiered base incentives have successfully attracted users with larger deposits, resulting in an average lending deposit size of approximately $1,500 during the week. This trend supports Vaultka's long-term growth by attracting larger depositors.
phase4_actualsSummary: Incentives were distributed to vault and lending pool users, with additional one-time incentives provided to those depositing more than $1,000 to prevent sybil farming. Daily unique users have decreased to levels slightly above those before the LTIPP, which is expected. The previous peak in DAU was primarily due to the forced closing of positions with the new maturity date feature. The slightly higher DAU now indicates a moderate success of the grant in attracting new users, with approximately 200 new users during the bi-week period.
phase4_plannedChanges: The USDC Lending pool supply is growing, supporting the demand for our leverage vaults. We will continue the one-off incentive and tiered base approach for our lending pools to keep incentivizing lenders. More focus will be put on USDC.e pools due to their rapid demand and high utilization rate.
phase4_plannedExpectations: Maintain the supply of USDC lending pool, while pumping up deposits in USDC.e Lending pool, as the utilization rate of its remain >95% most of the time.
phase5_statsChanges: 3000 ARB is allocated to incentivize wrapped SOL lending pool on Arbitrum, to incentivize the initial deposits of wrapped SOL lending pool, and support the leverage activities from GM Leverage, and GM Leverage (Neutral) vault, for GM-SOL pair.
phase5_statsLessons: Using incentives to support the launch of new demanding vaults have been proven successful as an initial suppport and attraction of liquidity. This help to enhance and support the leverage activities in strategic vault, supporting new pairs for trading.
phase5_actualsSummary:
The Wrapped SOL Lending Pool was successfully launched on Vaultka Arbitrum, along with the GM-SOL Leverage and Leverage (Neutral) vaults. This launch contributed an additional ~$700k to Vaultka’s TVL, driven by the growing popularity of SOL. To incentivize initial deposits in the Wrapped SOL pool, 3,000 ARB was allocated to the lending pool, which effectively attracted new capital. The pool’s utilization rate consistently remained above 90%, indicating strong demand for leveraged Wrapped SOL products. This performance demonstrates that the ARB allocation effectively helped Vaultka meet user demand.

A signficant amount of positsions are liquidated during the sudden market downturn during the biweek, leading to many leveraged positions being liquidateed. Borrowed funds are successfully returned to lending pool. Some lenders have then withdrawn their funds during the market downturn, probably as a protective gesture to finance his other positions. Thus, Vaultka's TVL dropped by $3-4 million.
phase5_actualsDisclosures: Snapshots for the incentives are taken on Wednesday 14th August. But since our multi-sig signer was away of office during the week, the distribution of ARB token for this Bi-week is postponed to 19th August. An announcement was released to the community, informing our users in advance that the distribution will be done on 19th.
phase5_plannedChanges: The demand for our leverage vaults is strong, with USDC lending pool utilization hovering around 95% in the past weeks. To maintain a healthy balance, we aim to keep the utilization rate at ~90%. Thus, incentives will continue for USDC and USDC.e vaults, keeping the healthiness of lending pools. Incentives will also be allocated to the wrapped SOL lending pool on Arbitrum, which will be launching next week, to incentivize initial deposits in the pool.
phase5_plannedExpectations: Due to the initial lower tvl of wrapped SOL lending pool, the ARB APR for this pool is expected to spike, attracting more SOL holders to bridge or wrap their assets onto Solana to receive more yields.
phase6_statsChanges: No changes applied to the grant framework during the week.
phase6_statsLessons: ARB incentives continue to demonstrate their appeal by attracting TVL to our lending pools, albeit with slower growth. To retain users once the ARB incentives conclude, Vaultka will adjust the lending rates to maintain competitiveness. Balancing the interests of lenders and borrowers may present challenges, especially as Perp DEX LP yields on Arbitrum trend downward. This decline in borrower rewards will impact the reward sharing with our lenders under our reward split mechanism.
phase6_actualsSummary: TVL on Arbitrum slowly grows, after the market drop and liquidations from last biweek. The incentivized USDC Lending pool grows gradually from $4m to $5.2m, while USDC.e Lending pool maintains at around $3m pool size. The demand from leverage strategies remain high, with Utilization rate maintaining at >95% most of the time. 3000 ARB is continued to incentivize the wrapped SOL lending pool on Arbitrum, maintaining its pool size at roughly $500k.
phase6_plannedChanges: The next bi-week marks the end of the LTIPP campaign. The final ARB allocations will continue to incentivize deposits into the USDC and USDC.e lending pools, with a small portion going to vault users to support demand.
phase6_plannedExpectations: The demand for leverage strategic vaults should continue to grow. The utilization rate of USDC lending pool has been staying for ~88%. These idle lending capacity is expected to be used up soon with the continuous rise of demand from leverage vaults.

phase7_plannedChanges: No ARB tokens are distributed this biweek.
phase7_plannedExpectations: Vaultka's Lending pool size to maintain at LTIPP level.

grantSummary: Yield Yak has received 195,000 ARB.  15,000 ARB to be awarded as gas refunds for Yak Swap users, and up to 180,000 ARB to be distributed to depositors in selected Yield Yak DeFi strategies.  ARB rewards increase as key TVL milestones are reached.
allContractsIncentivized: Llamapay streaming contract: 0xe99A1868cAb3277892710978E77a44aE26536394; Yield Yak: STG S*ETH: 0x4be9D486331ae4532C32000F6DCb62F94299a1CB; Yield Yak: STG S*USDC: 0xa1568767975c73dc9a3bb1ffc3651fc3cbb2ea23; Yield Yak: STG S*USDT: 0xa03d11b8c719b8aeb5e23cf79137c3454dcf21f5
phase1_statsChanges: We saw a huge increase in TVL for our Arbitrum strategies, going from appox. $1.2M before the incentives started to over $6M by the end of this 2 week period. The amount of YAK token bridged to Arbitrum also went up from under 500 (5% of total supply) to over 1300 (13% of total supply), which confirms our thoughts that a large amount of the extra TVL has been bridged from Avalanche.
phase1_statsLessons: These strategies are very popular and there is a lot of potential to attract significant TVL from other chains. In line with this, a number of new product features have been rolled out with Arbitrum in mind which improves visibility of cross-chain strategies.  Most notably a new "explore" tab on our strategy details pages which highlights other well-performing strategies on other chains with the same underlying deposit token. e.g. A Yield Yak Avalanche Stargate depositor will now see the equivalent Arbitrum farm higlighted to him while on Avalanche.
phase1_actualsSummary: ARB rewards started streaming on 5th June 3:46PM UTC. $2.5M TVL milestone was hit on the first day that rewards were active and $5M TVL milestone was hit within the first week of rewards streaming.
phase1_actualsDisclosures: No disclosures to add
phase1_contractsIncentivized: Llamapay streaming contract: 0xe99A1868cAb3277892710978E77a44aE26536394; Yield Yak: STG S*ETH: 0x4be9D486331ae4532C32000F6DCb62F94299a1CB; Yield Yak: STG S*USDT: 0xa03d11b8c719b8aeb5e23cf79137c3454dcf21f5; Yield Yak: STG S*USDC: 0xa1568767975c73dc9a3bb1ffc3651fc3cbb2ea23
phase1_plannedChanges: No changes compared to original proposal. ARB is distributed to DeFi strategies listed above using a Llamapay stream with the rate determined by TVL milestones. Yak Swap gas refunds will be distributed every 2 weeks - the start date was 5th June so first Airdrop will be after 19th June.
phase1_plannedExpectations: Expectation is that we will quickly achieve TVL milestones so that the highest streaming rate is activated.
phase1_plannedContracts: Llamapay streaming contract: 0xe99A1868cAb3277892710978E77a44aE26536394; Yield Yak: STG S*ETH: 0x4be9D486331ae4532C32000F6DCb62F94299a1CB; Yield Yak: STG S*USDT: 0xa03d11b8c719b8aeb5e23cf79137c3454dcf21f5; Yield Yak: STG S*USDC: 0xa1568767975c73dc9a3bb1ffc3651fc3cbb2ea23
phase2_statsChanges: We saw continued growth in the TVL of our strategies, but less so than in the first two weeks. The amount of YAK bridged over to Arbitrum also increased by 20%+ in the latest two weeks. The number of transactions has dropped a little but DAU remains at approximately the same level as for the first two weeks. We also recently added our new "explore" feature onto our Yak Swap Dex Aggregator page, to improve visibility of high-yielding strategies (such as those being incentivised by the LTIPP).
phase2_statsLessons: Despite significantly higher real yield for the YAK token on Arbitrum, and a pretty easy process, the amount bridged over remains lower than expected. We are going to make some changes to make the process even easier and add the option to convert some of their bridged YAK to ETH during the process in order to pay for gas and make Arbitrum as a whole more attractive to Avalanche users. In addition, we are looking at new strategies to add based on LTIPP/STIP.
phase2_actualsSummary: Top level of rewards has been maintained due to sticky TVL remaining above the $5M threshold. First round of Yak Swap refunds were issued during this period also.

phase1_statsChanges: No changes were applied over the last 2 weeks.

phase1_actualsSummary: For this two-week phase there will be no airdrop distribution. The first one will start the 7th July.
phase1_actualsDisclosures: Mechanism for distribution incentives: For each monthly epoch, we will use a multi-send tool to send the rewards to the eligible users from the Funding multi-sig address, as we don’t want to introduce extra risk in developing a smart contract to send the reward.
phase1_plannedChanges: None
phase1_plannedExpectations: Since we started the incentives on 11/06 we do not expect major changes in this period.

phase2_statsChanges: None
phase2_statsLessons:
Incentives are working as expected, especially the trading competition.
The TVL increased and the daily trading volume nearly doubled from $37,692 to $61 ,898.
The first distribution of incentive will happen next period which will be interesting to see if current users will continue to participate to next trading competition.
phase2_actualsSummary: The Trading Competition and incentives are attracting traders and investors on perpetual trading vaults.
phase2_actualsDisclosures: The TVL is higher at 112,616$ with a constant open interest on GMX of 202,847$ according to the Dune Dashboard.
phase2_plannedChanges: None
phase2_plannedExpectations: With the beginning of the incentives and the GMX Trading Competition, an increase in TVL and volume is expected.
phase3_statsChanges: None
phase3_statsLessons: The dApp is very depend to the market and the traders. Despite the market crash, the number of transactions and unique user nearly doubled on this period.
phase3_actualsSummary: The protocol depends a lot on market conditions and on traders.
phase3_actualsDisclosures: The TVL ($119k to $90k) and open interest($130k to $50k) dropped due the market crash.
phase3_plannedChanges: None
phase3_plannedExpectations: This period might be complicated due to the market. However the end of the first competition might also bring more volume.
phase4_statsChanges: The winnings of the second competition have been revised upwards to attract more traders.
phase4_statsLessons: Traders in the dApp aim for longer term trades. It has been thought that this behavior comes from fees, however even with ARB compensation of fees the way of trading remains the same. That is why it seems better to reward good traders through competitions
phase4_actualsSummary: The ARB allocated for rebates will also be used to more reward great traders through the competition.
phase4_actualsDisclosures: Traders seem to privilege higher time frame trades despite the rebates proposed thanks to the grant.
phase4_plannedChanges: The rewards of the trading competition will be higher during this epoch to continue to attracts traders.
phase4_plannedExpectations: With the increase in rewards a growth in volume is expected.
phase5_statsChanges: The incentives regarding the TVL of each are not effective enough as well as for the reduction of costs and should be modified. A part of the ARB which was initially allocated for these two parties will be used for the last two competitions.
phase5_statsLessons:
Competitions remain the most effective incentive.
However, the choice of having one competition per month does not allow traders to return regularly to the dApp. This is why for the last month of the grant two competitions will be made.
phase5_actualsSummary: Daily volume more than doubled from 55k to 121K and TVL did not decline much despite market conditions.
phase5_actualsDisclosures: The second competition has ended and the increase in competition rewards has helped attract more volume despite the complicated market conditions.
phase5_plannedChanges: No changes.
phase5_plannedExpectations: With the end of the second competition an increase in volume is expected
phase6_statsLessons: Although the market is still in a consolidation phase, users appreciate the simpler, shorter-term application of incentives. TVL increased by 10%, as did the number of vaults.
phase6_actualsSummary: There is just one last distrubition to be made, which will take place at the end of the last competition.
phase6_actualsDisclosures: The first competition ended and the distribution took place.
phase6_plannedChanges: None
phase6_plannedExpectations: With the announcement of two shorter competitions at the end of the month, tvl and the number of users should increase.
phase7_statsChanges: None
phase7_statsLessons: Running an incentive campaign was rewarding and gave us a better understanding of how to attract more users to the especially through competitions platform.
phase7_actualsSummary: September 1st distribution concludes LTIPP
phase7_actualsDisclosures: ARB were distributed on September 1st concerning the 3 following points: the trading competition, the reduction fees as well as the bonus for vaults which respected TVL and volume instructions.
phase7_plannedChanges: The 20,000 ARB intended to reward vaults that have fulfilled specific tasks at the end of the LTIPP will be distributed equally.
phase7_plannedExpectations: More volume and TVL thanks to the last trading competition.
